def_en: feeling pleased about one's own or someone's achievements or possessions (proud to say, proud of, proud to be)
def_zh: 自豪的；骄傲的（自豪地说，为……而骄傲，自豪成为）
quote_en: She felt proud when her younger brother won the school spelling bee.
quote_zh: 当她的弟弟赢得学校拼写比赛时，她感到自豪。
ex_en: The Dursleys were proud to say they were perfectly normal, never realizing how dull that made them.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家自豪地说他们完全正常，从未意识到这让他们显得多么乏味。

def_en: completely ordinary and unremarkable, often said with irony
def_zh: 完全正常的；极其普通的（常含讽刺）
quote_en: After the accident, he wanted everything to go back to perfectly normal.
quote_zh: 事故之后，他希望一切都恢复完全正常。
ex_en: The Dursleys insisted they were perfectly normal, but their fear of anything strange proved otherwise.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家坚称他们完全正常，但他们害怕任何奇怪事物的态度恰恰证明了相反。

def_en: being part of something or connected with it (be involved in something, get involved)
def_zh: 参与的；卷入的（参与某事，卷入）
quote_en: She didn't want to be involved in the argument between her friends.
quote_zh: 她不想卷入朋友之间的争吵。
ex_en: The Dursleys were the last people you'd expect to be involved in anything strange or mysterious.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家是你最不可能预料会卷入任何奇怪或神秘事情的人。

def_en: to approve of or agree with something, usually used in negative statements (not hold with something)
def_zh: 赞同；认可（通常用于否定句）（不赞同某事）
quote_en: My grandfather doesn't hold with using smartphones at the dinner table.
quote_zh: 我祖父不赞同在餐桌上使用智能手机。
ex_en: The Dursleys didn't hold with such nonsense, which in their minds included magic, wizards, and anything unDursleyish.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家不赞同这类胡说八道，在他们看来这包括魔法、巫师以及任何不像德思礼家的事物。

def_en: a person who manages or controls a company or organization (company director, board director)
def_zh: 主管；经理；董事（公司主管，董事会董事）
quote_en: The director of the hospital announced new safety rules.
quote_zh: 医院院长宣布了新的安全规定。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley was the director of a firm called Grunnings, which made drills.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生是一家叫格朗宁的公司的主管，那家公司生产钻机。

def_en: a business company, especially a small or medium‑sized one (law firm, accounting firm)
def_zh: 公司；商行（律师事务所，会计事务所）
quote_en: She works for a large consulting firm in the city center.
quote_zh: 她在市中心一家大型咨询公司工作。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley's firm, Grunnings, manufactured drills, the most boring product imaginable.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生的公司格朗宁生产钻机，这是能想象到的最无聊的产品。

def_en: tools or machines used for making holes in hard materials (power drill, electric drill)
quote_en: The construction workers used heavy drills to break through the concrete.
quote_zh: 建筑工人用重型钻机打穿混凝土。
ex_en: The Dursleys' world revolved around drills, which perfectly matched their unmagical, practical lives.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家的世界围绕着钻机，这完全符合他们非魔法的、务实的生活。

def_en: large, strong, and heavily built (beefy arms, beefy build)
def_zh: 结实的；粗壮的（粗壮的手臂，结实的身材）
quote_en: The beefy security guard stood at the entrance.
quote_zh: 那个结实的保安站在入口处。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley was a big, beefy man with hardly any neck and a very large mustache.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生是个高大结实的男人，几乎没有脖子，却留着非常大的胡子。

def_en: very little or almost none
quote_en: There was hardly any milk left in the fridge.
quote_zh: 冰箱里几乎没有牛奶了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley was a beefy man with hardly any neck, making him look like a large walrus.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生是个结实的男人，几乎没有脖子，让他看起来像一头大海象。

def_en: hair grown on the upper lip (thick mustache, handlebar mustache)
def_zh: 胡子；小胡子（浓密的小胡子，翘胡子）
quote_en: The old man twirled his mustache while thinking.
quote_zh: 那位老人一边思考一边捻着胡子。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley had a very large mustache, which he nervously stroked when worried about the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生留着非常大的胡子，当他担心波特一家时就会紧张地抚摸它。

def_en: having little fat or flesh; not thick (thin face, thin arms)
quote_en: She was thin after months of dieting.
quote_zh: 经过几个月的节食，她变瘦了。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley was thin and blonde, the complete opposite of her beefy husband.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人又瘦又 blonde（金发），与她结实的丈夫完全相反。

def_en: having yellow or golden hair (blonde hair, blonde woman)
quote_en: Her blonde hair shone in the summer sun.
quote_zh: 她的金发在夏日的阳光下闪耀。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley was thin and blonde, spending much of her time spying on neighbors.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人又瘦又金发，花大量时间窥探邻居。

def_en: two times the quantity of something
quote_en: You need twice the amount of flour to make two cakes.
quote_zh: 你需要两倍的面粉来做两个蛋糕。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley had nearly twice the usual amount of neck, which helped her spy on everyone.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人的脖子几乎是通常的两倍长，这帮助她窥探所有人。

def_en: proved to be helpful or convenient (come in useful, come in handy)
def_zh: 派上用场；有用处（派上用场，派上用场）
quote_en: The extra batteries came in useful during the long power cut.
quote_zh: 备用电池在长时间停电时派上了用场。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley's long neck came in very useful for looking over garden fences without being seen.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人的长脖子在越过花园栅栏而不被看见时非常有用。

def_en: to use a period of time on a specific activity
quote_en: Many students spend time doing homework after school.
quote_zh: 许多学生放学后花时间做作业。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley spent so much of her time craning over fences that her neck grew unusually long.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人花了那么多时间越过栅栏伸长脖子，结果她的脖子变得异常长。

def_en: to stretch one's neck forward to see something better (crane over, crane forward, crane one's neck)
def_zh: 伸长（脖子）；探头（伸长脖子越过……，向前探头，伸长某人的脖子）
quote_en: The audience craned forward to catch a glimpse of the famous singer.
quote_zh: 观众向前探头想看一眼那位著名歌手。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley spent hours craning over garden fences, watching everything the neighbors did.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人花几个小时越过花园栅栏伸长脖子，观察邻居做的一切。

def_en: to watch someone secretly, often to learn private information (spy on someone, spy for a country)
def_zh: 窥探；暗中监视（窥探某人，为某个国家做间谍）
quote_en: The children liked to spy on their older sister through the keyhole.
quote_zh: 孩子们喜欢透过钥匙孔偷看他们的姐姐。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley used her long neck to spy on the neighbors, noticing every detail of their lives.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人用她的长脖子窥探邻居，注意到他们生活中每一个细节。

def_en: used in negative comparatives to mean "the best possible" (no finer boy, no finer example)
def_zh: 没有更好的；最好的（没有更好的男孩，没有更好的例子）
quote_en: In her eyes, there was no finer gift than a handmade card.
quote_zh: 在她眼里，没有比手工卡片更好的礼物了。
ex_en: The Dursleys believed there was no finer boy anywhere than their son Dudley.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家相信世界上没有比他们儿子达力更好的孩子了。

def_en: used to show that a statement is someone's personal belief or judgment (in my opinion, in his/her opinion)
def_zh: 在某人看来；依某人看（在我看来，依他/她的看法）
quote_en: In my opinion, this restaurant serves the best noodles in town.
quote_zh: 在我看来，这家餐馆供应城里最好的面条。
ex_en: In the Dursleys' opinion, Dudley was the finest boy who had ever lived.
ex_zh: 在德思礼一家看来，达力是有史以来最好的孩子。

def_en: something kept hidden or known only to a few people (have a secret, discover a secret, keep a secret)
def_zh: 秘密（有秘密，发现秘密，保守秘密）
quote_en: She promised never to reveal her friend's secret.
quote_zh: 她承诺永远不会泄露朋友的秘密。
ex_en: The Dursleys had a secret: Mrs. Dursley's sister was a witch, and they were terrified anyone would discover it.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家有一个秘密：德思礼夫人的妹妹是个女巫，他们害怕任何人会发现这件事。

def_en: the thing someone is most afraid of (one's greatest fear, the greatest fear is that)
def_zh: 最大的恐惧（某人最大的恐惧，最大的恐惧是……）
quote_en: His greatest fear was speaking in front of a large audience.
quote_zh: 他最大的恐惧是在一大群观众面前讲话。
ex_en: The Dursleys' greatest fear was that someone would find out about the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家最大的恐惧是有人会发现波特一家的存在。

def_en: to tolerate or endure something unpleasant, usually with "can't" (can't bear it, bear to do something)
def_zh: 忍受；容忍（无法忍受，忍受做某事）
quote_en: She couldn't bear the noise from the construction site.
quote_zh: 她无法忍受建筑工地的噪音。
ex_en: The Dursleys didn't think they could bear it if anyone found out about the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家认为如果有人发现波特一家的事，他们会无法忍受。

def_en: to discover information, especially something hidden or secret
quote_en: The children tried to find out about the surprise party.
quote_zh: 孩子们试图查明惊喜派对的事。
ex_en: The Dursleys lived in constant fear that the neighbors might find out about Mrs. Dursley's sister.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家一直生活在恐惧中，生怕邻居会发现德思礼夫人的妹妹的事。

def_en: to act as if something is true when it is not (pretend that, pretend to do)
quote_en: The boy pretended to be sick so he could stay home.
quote_zh: 那个男孩假装生病以便待在家里。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley pretended she didn't have a sister because the truth was too embarrassing.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人假装没有妹妹，因为真相太令人尴尬了。

def_en: worthless; having no useful qualities (good-for-nothing husband, good-for-nothing lazy person)
def_zh: 没用的；一无是处的（没用的丈夫，没用的懒汉）
quote_en: That good-for-nothing old car broke down again.
quote_zh: 那辆没用的旧车又抛锚了。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley called her sister's husband a good-for-nothing wizard.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人称她妹妹的丈夫是个没用的巫师。

def_en: not like the Dursleys; opposite of their normal, boring, magic‑hating nature
def_zh: 不像德思礼家的；与德思礼家格格不入的
quote_en: His behavior was so unDursleyish that everyone stared in shock.
quote_zh: 他的行为如此不像德思礼家的人，所有人都震惊地看着。
ex_en: The Potters were as unDursleyish as it was possible to be — they loved magic and everything strange.
ex_zh: 波特一家简直不能再不像德思礼家的人了——他们热爱魔法和一切奇怪的事物。

def_en: to be very afraid or horrified to imagine something
def_zh: 一想到……就不寒而栗；不敢想象
quote_en: I shudder to think what would happen if the bridge collapsed.
quote_zh: 我一想到桥会塌就不寒而栗。
ex_en: The Dursleys shuddered to think what the neighbors would say if the Potters ever arrived on Privet Drive.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家一想到如果波特一家出现在女贞路上邻居们会说什么，就不寒而栗。

def_en: to reach a place, especially a city, town, street, or area (arrive in a street, arrive in a city)
def_zh: 到达（某地，尤指街道、城市等范围）（到达某条街，到达某个城市）
quote_en: They will arrive in New York early tomorrow morning.
quote_zh: 他们明天一早将到达纽约。
ex_en: The Dursleys were terrified that the Potters might arrive in their street.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家害怕波特一家会出现在他们的街上。

def_en: to prevent someone or something from coming near (keep someone away, keep away from)
def_zh: 使远离；不让接近（不让某人接近，远离）
quote_en: The fence is meant to keep wild animals away from the garden.
quote_zh: 这栅栏是为了防止野生动物靠近花园。
ex_en: The Dursleys had a good reason for keeping the Potters away: they didn't want Dudley mixing with a magical child.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家有充分的理由不让波特一家靠近：他们不想让达力和一个会魔法的孩子混在一起。

def_en: to associate or socialize with someone, often implying disapproval
def_zh: 与……交往；与……混在一起（常含贬义）
quote_en: His parents didn't want him mixing with the older troublemakers.
quote_zh: 他的父母不想让他和那些年长的捣蛋鬼混在一起。
ex_en: The Dursleys didn't want Dudley mixing with a child like Harry Potter.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家不想让达力和像哈利·波特那样的孩子混在一起。

def_en: to stop sleeping; to become awake (wake up, woke up)
quote_en: I usually wake up at seven o'clock on weekdays.
quote_zh: 我通常在工作日七点醒来。
ex_en: Mr. and Mrs. Dursley woke up on a dull, gray Tuesday, completely unaware that strange things were happening across the country.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫妇在一个阴沉灰暗的星期二醒来，完全不知道奇怪的事情正在全国发生。

def_en: not bright, clear, or interesting; boring (dull color, dull weather, dull person)
def_zh: 阴沉的；暗淡的；乏味的（暗淡的颜色，阴沉的天气，乏味的人）
quote_en: The sky was dull and gray before the rain started.
quote_zh: 下雨前天空阴沉灰暗。
ex_en: The story begins on a dull, gray Tuesday, but by nightfall everything would change.
ex_zh: 故事从一个阴沉灰暗的星期二开始，但到了晚上一切都会改变。

def_en: to imply or indicate that something is true or exists (suggest that, suggest something)
def_zh: 表明；暗示（表明……，暗示某事）
quote_en: The dark clouds suggest that a storm is coming.
quote_zh: 乌云表明暴风雨即将来临。
ex_en: Nothing about the cloudy sky outside suggested that strange and mysterious things would soon be happening.
ex_zh: 外面阴沉的天空丝毫没有表明奇怪而神秘的事情即将发生。

def_en: unusual, odd, and difficult to explain or understand
quote_en: The old house had a strange and mysterious atmosphere.
quote_zh: 那座老房子有一种奇怪而神秘的氛围。
ex_en: The Dursleys wanted nothing to do with anything strange and mysterious.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家不想与任何奇怪而神秘的事物有任何关系。

def_en: everywhere within a nation; throughout the entire country
quote_en: People all over the country celebrated the holiday.
quote_zh: 全国各地的人们都在庆祝这个节日。
ex_en: Strange and mysterious things would soon be happening all over the country, though the Dursleys knew nothing of it.
ex_zh: 奇怪而神秘的事情即将在全国各地发生，尽管德思礼一家对此一无所知。

def_en: to choose or select something from a group (pick out, pick out a tie)
quote_en: She picked out the ripest apples at the market.
quote_zh: 她在市场上挑选了最熟的苹果。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley hummed as he picked out his most boring tie for work.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生一边哼着歌，一边挑选他最无聊的领带去上班。

def_en: to talk idly about other people's private lives for a period of time
quote_en: The neighbors gossiped away for hours on the porch.
quote_zh: 邻居们在门廊上闲聊了好几个小时。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley gossiped away happily as she wrestled a screaming Dudley into his high chair.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人一边愉快地闲聊，一边把一个尖叫的达力按进他的高脚椅。

def_en: to struggle physically with someone or something; to force something into a position with difficulty (wrestle with, wrestle into)
def_zh: 用力对付；挣扎着把……弄进
quote_en: The mother wrestled the heavy suitcase into the car trunk.
quote_zh: 那位母亲费力地把沉重的行李箱塞进汽车后备箱。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley wrestled a screaming Dudley into his high chair, a daily battle she seemed to enjoy.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人把一个尖叫的达力按进他的高脚椅，这是一场她似乎乐在其中的日常战斗。

def_en: making a loud, high-pitched cry of pain, fear, or anger (screaming child, screaming voice)
def_zh: 尖叫的（尖叫的孩子，尖叫的声音）
quote_en: The screaming baby kept everyone awake all night.
quote_zh: 尖叫的婴儿让所有人整夜没睡。
ex_en: Dudley was a screaming toddler who threw cereal at the walls whenever he didn't get his way.
ex_zh: 达力是个尖叫的幼儿，只要不顺心就把麦片扔到墙上。

def_en: not a single person or thing from a particular group
def_zh: 他们中没有一个；没有一个人
quote_en: None of them knew the answer to the difficult question.
quote_zh: 他们中没有人知道那个难题的答案。
ex_en: None of them noticed the large, tawny owl flutter past the window.
ex_zh: 他们没有一个人注意到一只大的茶色猫头鹰从窗前飞过。

def_en: saw or became aware of something (notice someone do something, notice that)
def_zh: 注意到；留意到（注意到某人做某事，注意到……）
quote_en: She noticed a stranger standing at the door.
quote_zh: 她注意到一个陌生人站在门口。
ex_en: Mr. and Mrs. Dursley did not notice the owl flutter past, because they never looked up from their normal lives.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫妇没有注意到猫头鹰飞过，因为他们从未从正常生活中抬起头来。

def_en: brownish-yellow in color (tawny owl, tawny fur)
def_zh: 茶色的；黄褐色的（茶色猫头鹰，黄褐色的毛皮）
quote_en: The lion's tawny coat helped it blend into the dry grass.
quote_zh: 狮子黄褐色的皮毛帮助它融入干草丛中。
ex_en: A large, tawny owl flew past the Dursleys' window in broad daylight.
ex_zh: 一只大的茶色猫头鹰在大白天飞过德思礼家的窗户。

def_en: a bird of prey that usually hunts at night, often associated with wisdom and magic (tawny owl, barn owl)
def_zh: 猫头鹰（茶色猫头鹰，仓鸮）
quote_en: The owl sat silently on the tree branch, watching the ground below.
quote_zh: 猫头鹰静静地坐在树枝上，看着下面的地面。
ex_en: Owls are common in the magical world but rarely seen by Muggles like the Dursleys.
ex_zh: 猫头鹰在魔法世界很常见，但像德思礼家这样的麻瓜很少见到。

def_en: to fly lightly and quickly past something
quote_en: A small butterfly fluttered past my face.
quote_zh: 一只小蝴蝶从我脸前轻轻飞过。
ex_en: None of the Dursleys noticed the tawny owl flutter past their window.
ex_zh: 没有一个德思礼家的人注意到那只茶色猫头鹰从他们窗前飞过。

def_en: to lift or take something from a surface or place; to collect (pick up a briefcase, pick up a child)
def_zh: 拿起；捡起；接载（拿起公文包，接孩子）
quote_en: She picked up the phone and dialed her mother's number.
quote_zh: 她拿起电话拨了妈妈的号码。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley picked up his briefcase and pecked his wife on the cheek before leaving.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生拿起公文包，在妻子脸颊上轻吻了一下，然后离开了。

def_en: gave a quick, light kiss (peck someone on the cheek)
def_zh: 匆匆轻吻（轻吻某人的脸颊）
quote_en: The old man pecked his granddaughter on the forehead.
quote_zh: 老人在孙女的额头上轻吻了一下。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley pecked Mrs. Dursley on the cheek, then tried to kiss Dudley but missed.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生匆匆轻吻了妻子一下，然后试图亲吻达力但没亲到。

def_en: having a sudden, angry outburst of crying and bad behavior, typical of young children
quote_en: The toddler started having a tantrum when his mother said no to candy.
quote_zh: 当妈妈拒绝给糖果时，那个幼儿开始发脾气。
ex_en: Dudley was having a tantrum, throwing his cereal at the walls instead of eating it.
ex_zh: 达力正在发脾气，把麦片扔到墙上而不是吃掉。

def_en: an affectionate term for a small child, especially one who is mischievous or lively
def_zh: 小家伙（常指调皮的小孩）
quote_en: Come here, you little tyke, and give me a hug.
quote_zh: 过来，你这个小家伙，给我抱抱。
ex_en: "Little tyke," chortled Mr. Dursley as Dudley screamed and threw cereal everywhere.
ex_zh: "小家伙，"德思礼先生咯咯笑着说，此时达力尖叫着把麦片扔得到处都是。

def_en: laughed with joy or amusement, often with a snorting sound
quote_en: The old man chortled at the funny cartoon.
quote_zh: 老人看着有趣的卡通片咯咯地笑。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley chortled with pride at his son's bad behavior, calling him a little tyke.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生对儿子的坏行为感到自豪，咯咯地笑着叫他小家伙。

def_en: reversed a vehicle out of a space (back out of a driveway, back out of a garage)
quote_en: He carefully backed out of the narrow parking space.
quote_zh: 他小心地从狭窄的停车位倒车出来。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley got into his car and backed out of number four's driveway.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生上了车，从四号车道上倒车出来。

def_en: strange or unusual, often in a way that is surprising (peculiar behavior, peculiar sign)
def_zh: 奇怪的；异常的（奇怪的行为，奇怪的迹象）
quote_en: There was a peculiar smell coming from the kitchen.
quote_zh: 厨房里传来一股奇怪的气味。
ex_en: The first sign of something peculiar was a cat reading a map on the corner.
ex_zh: 第一个异常迹象是一只猫在拐角处看地图。

def_en: looking at and understanding a map to find directions
quote_en: The tourist was reading a map to find the museum.
quote_zh: 那位游客正在看地图找博物馆。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley saw a cat reading a map, which was impossible because cats can't read.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生看到一只猫在看地图，这是不可能的，因为猫不会阅读。

def_en: turned or moved his head suddenly and quickly
quote_en: She jerked her head around when she heard someone call her name.
quote_zh: 当她听到有人叫她的名字时，她猛地转过头。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley jerked his head around to look again at the cat, but the map had disappeared.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生猛地转过头再看那只猫，但地图已经消失了。

def_en: a domestic cat with a striped or spotted coat
quote_en: The tabby cat slept in the sun all afternoon.
quote_zh: 那只虎斑猫整个下午都在阳光下睡觉。
ex_en: The tabby cat on Privet Drive was actually Professor McGonagall in disguise.
ex_zh: 女贞路上的那只虎斑猫其实是麦格教授伪装的。

def_en: visible; able to be seen
quote_en: There was no other car in sight on the empty road.
quote_zh: 空旷的道路上看不见其他车辆。
ex_en: When Mr. Dursley looked again, there wasn't a map in sight anywhere.
ex_zh: 当德思礼先生再看时，哪里都看不到地图了。

def_en: an optical illusion caused by the way light falls on something
def_zh: 光线造成的错觉；视觉欺骗
quote_en: The shadow on the wall looked like a face, but it was just a trick of the light.
quote_zh: 墙上的影子看起来像一张脸，但那只是光线造成的错觉。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley told himself the cat reading a map must have been a trick of the light.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生告诉自己那只猫看地图一定是光线造成的错觉。

def_en: closed and opened both eyes quickly
quote_en: She blinked several times to clear her blurry vision.
quote_zh: 她眨了几次眼以消除模糊的视线。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley blinked and stared at the cat, which stared back without blinking.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生眨了眨眼，盯着那只猫，而那只猫也盯着他，眼睛一眨不眨。

def_en: looked at someone or something for a long time with fixed eyes
quote_en: The children stared at the magician with amazement.
quote_zh: 孩子们惊奇地凝视着魔术师。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley stared at the cat, and the cat stared back at him.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生盯着那只猫，那只猫也盯着他。

def_en: physically shook one's body slightly to regain composure or clear one's mind
def_zh: 微微摇了摇头（以恢复镇定）
quote_en: He gave himself a little shake and continued walking.
quote_zh: 他微微摇了摇头，继续往前走。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley gave himself a little shake and decided to forget about the cat.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生微微摇了摇头，决定忘掉那只猫。

def_en: deliberately stopped thinking about something
quote_en: She tried to put the bad news out of her mind and focus on work.
quote_zh: 她努力把坏消息抛到脑后，专注于工作。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley put the strange cat out of his mind and thought only about drills.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生把那只有怪异的猫抛到脑后，只想着钻机。

def_en: forced something to leave one's thoughts; replaced in one's attention
quote_en: The loud music drove all thoughts of studying out of my mind.
quote_zh: 大声的音乐把所有学习的念头都赶出了我的脑海。
ex_en: Drills were driven out of Mr. Dursley's mind by the sight of strangely dressed people.
ex_zh: 钻机被穿着奇怪的人们从德思礼先生的脑海中赶走了。

def_en: a long line of vehicles that cannot move or moves very slowly
quote_en: I was late for work because of a traffic jam on the highway.
quote_zh: 因为高速公路上堵车，我上班迟到了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley sat in the usual morning traffic jam, drumming his fingers impatiently.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生像往常一样早晨堵在路上，不耐烦地用手指敲击着。

def_en: was unable to avoid seeing or becoming aware of something
def_zh: 忍不住注意到；不禁注意到
quote_en: She couldn't help noticing that her friend looked sad.
quote_zh: 她忍不住注意到她的朋友看起来很难过。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley couldn't help noticing that there seemed to be a lot of strangely dressed people about.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生忍不住注意到周围似乎有很多穿着奇怪的人。

def_en: wearing unusual or odd clothing
quote_en: The strangely dressed man stood out in the crowd.
quote_zh: 那个穿着奇怪的男人在人群中很显眼。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley saw strangely dressed people in cloaks everywhere in town.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生在镇上到处看到穿着奇怪斗篷的人。

def_en: outfits or costumes, especially strange or unusual ones
quote_en: I can't believe the getups some people wear to parties.
quote_zh: 我真不敢相信有些人穿着那样的奇装异服去派对。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley hated the getups young people wore, and the cloaks were even worse.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生讨厌年轻人穿的奇装异服，而斗篷更糟。

def_en: tapped his fingers repeatedly on a surface, usually from impatience or boredom
def_zh: 用手指敲击（表示不耐烦）
quote_en: The student drummed his fingers on the desk while waiting for the bell.
quote_zh: 那个学生等待铃响时用手指敲击桌子。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley drummed his fingers on the steering wheel, annoyed by the traffic and the weirdos.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生用手指敲击方向盘，对交通和那些怪人感到恼火。

def_en: the wheel that a driver turns to control the direction of a vehicle
quote_en: Keep both hands on the steering wheel while driving.
quote_zh: 开车时双手放在方向盘上。
ex_en: He drummed his fingers impatiently on the steering wheel.
ex_zh: 他不耐烦地用方向盘上敲击手指。

def_en: a small group of people standing close together
def_zh: 一小群人；挤在一起的一群人
quote_en: A huddle of fans waited outside the concert hall.
quote_zh: 一小群粉丝在音乐厅外等候。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley's eyes fell on a huddle of weirdos standing close by.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生的目光落在旁边站着的一小群怪人身上。

def_en: strange or eccentric people (informal, disapproving)
def_zh: 怪人；古怪的人（非正式，贬义）
quote_en: The neighborhood kids thought the old man was a weirdo.
quote_zh: 邻居的孩子们觉得那个老人是个怪人。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley called the people in cloaks weirdos, not realizing they were wizards.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生称那些穿斗篷的人为怪人，没意识到他们是巫师。

def_en: speaking very softly with enthusiasm or agitation
quote_en: The children were whispering excitedly about the presents under the tree.
quote_zh: 孩子们兴奋地窃窃私语着树下的礼物。
ex_en: The huddle of strangely dressed people were whispering excitedly together.
ex_zh: 那群穿着奇怪的人在一起兴奋地窃窃私语。

def_en: very angry; furious (enraged by something, become enraged)
quote_en: The driver was enraged by the constant honking.
quote_zh: 司机被不断的喇叭声激怒了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley was enraged to see an older man wearing an emerald-green cloak.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生看到一位比他年长的男人穿着翠绿色斗篷，非常愤怒。

def_en: bright green in color, like the gemstone emerald
quote_en: She wore an emerald-green dress to the party.
quote_zh: 她穿了一件翠绿色的裙子去参加派对。
ex_en: An old man wearing an emerald-green cloak hugged Mr. Dursley later that day.
ex_zh: 那天晚些时候，一位穿着翠绿色斗篷的老人拥抱了德思礼先生。

def_en: expression of outrage at someone's bold or rude behavior
quote_en: He cut in line right in front of me. The nerve of him!
quote_zh: 他就在我前面插队。他可真敢！
ex_en: Mr. Dursley thought, "The nerve of him!" when he saw an old man in a bright cloak.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生看到一位穿着亮色斗篷的老人时心想："他可真敢！"

def_en: (past of strike) occurred to; came into one's mind suddenly (it struck me that)
quote_en: It struck me that I had forgotten to lock the door.
quote_zh: 我突然意识到我忘了锁门。
ex_en: It struck Mr. Dursley that the people in cloaks might be collecting for something.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生突然想到，那些穿斗篷的人可能是在为某件事募捐。

def_en: a silly or dangerous act done for attention (silly stunt, publicity stunt)
quote_en: Jumping off the garage roof was a stupid stunt.
quote_zh: 从车库屋顶跳下来是愚蠢的噱头。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley told himself the whole thing was probably some silly stunt.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生告诉自己整件事可能只是某种愚蠢的噱头。

def_en: gathering money or goods for a charity or cause
quote_en: The volunteers were collecting for the local animal shelter.
quote_zh: 志愿者们正在为当地的动物收容所募捐。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley assumed the people in cloaks were collecting for something.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生以为那些穿斗篷的人是在为某件事募捐。

def_en: facing away from something; having one's back facing something
quote_en: He sat with his back to the door, so he didn't see who came in.
quote_zh: 他背对着门坐着，所以没看到谁进来了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley always sat with his back to the window, avoiding any distractions.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生总是背对着窗户坐着，避免任何干扰。

def_en: focus all one's attention on something
quote_en: Please concentrate on your homework instead of watching TV.
quote_zh: 请专注于你的作业，而不是看电视。
ex_en: If Mr. Dursley had faced the window, he would have found it harder to concentrate on drills.
ex_zh: 如果德思礼先生面朝窗户，他会发现更难专注于钻机。

def_en: flying quickly downward or in a curve past something
quote_en: The eagle swooped past the mountain ridge.
quote_zh: 鹰俯冲飞过山脊。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley didn't see the owls swooping past his window in broad daylight.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生没有看到猫头鹰在大白天从他的窗外俯冲飞过。

def_en: during the day, when it is light; openly, without hiding
def_zh: 在大白天；光天化日之下
quote_en: The robbery happened in broad daylight on a busy street.
quote_zh: 抢劫发生在光天化日之下的一条繁忙街道上。
ex_en: Owls, which normally hunt at night, were flying in broad daylight.
ex_zh: 通常在夜间捕食的猫头鹰正在大白天飞翔。

def_en: stared with one's mouth open in surprise or shock
quote_en: The crowd gazed open-mouthed as the magician made the elephant disappear.
quote_zh: 当魔术师让大象消失时，人群目瞪口呆地看着。
ex_en: People in the street pointed and gazed open-mouthed as owl after owl sped overhead.
ex_zh: 街上的人们指着天空，目瞪口呆地看着一只又一只猫头鹰从头顶飞过。

def_en: one owl following another; many owls in succession
quote_en: Owl after owl flew out of the forest at dusk.
quote_zh: 黄昏时分，一只又一只猫头鹰飞出森林。
ex_en: Owl after owl sped overhead, which no one had ever seen before.
ex_zh: 一只又一只猫头鹰从头顶飞过，这是从未有人见过的景象。

def_en: moved quickly above one's head
quote_en: The helicopter sped overhead, making a loud noise.
quote_zh: 直升机从头顶快速飞过，发出巨大的噪音。
ex_en: Owls sped overhead while Mr. Dursley remained unaware in his office.
ex_zh: 猫头鹰从头顶快速飞过，而德思礼先生在他的办公室里浑然不觉。

def_en: without any owls; not containing or bothered by owls
quote_en: It was an owl-free morning in the Dursley household.
quote_zh: 在德思礼家，这是一个没有猫头鹰的早晨。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley had a perfectly normal, owl-free morning.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生度过了一个完全正常的、没有猫头鹰的早晨。

def_en: shouted angrily at someone
quote_en: The coach yelled at the players for missing practice.
quote_zh: 教练对球员们大喊大叫，因为他们错过了训练。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley yelled at five different people before lunchtime.
ex_zh: 午餐前，德思礼先生对五个不同的人大喊大叫。

def_en: to walk around after sitting for a long time
quote_en: After sitting on the plane for six hours, I needed to stretch my legs.
quote_zh: 在飞机上坐了六个小时后，我需要活动一下腿脚。
ex_en: At lunchtime, Mr. Dursley thought he'd stretch his legs and walk to the bakery.
ex_zh: 午餐时，德思礼先生想活动一下腿脚，走到面包店去。

def_en: a small, round, sweet bread roll
quote_en: He bought a cinnamon bun from the bakery.
quote_zh: 他从面包店买了一个肉桂面包。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley walked across the road to buy himself a bun from the bakery.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生穿过马路去面包店给自己买一个小圆面包。

def_en: looked at someone or something with suspicion or interest (eyed angrily, eyed suspiciously)
def_zh: 注视着；打量着（生气地注视着，怀疑地打量着）
quote_en: The security guard eyed the stranger with suspicion.
quote_zh: 保安怀疑地打量着陌生人。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley eyed the group in cloaks angrily as he passed them.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生经过那群穿斗篷的人时，生气地注视着他们。

def_en: caused him to feel worried, nervous, or uncomfortable
def_zh: 让他不安；使他感到不自在
quote_en: The strange silence in the house made him uneasy.
quote_zh: 房子里奇怪的寂静让他不安。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley didn't know why, but the people in cloaks made him uneasy.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生不知道为什么，但那些穿斗篷的人让他感到不安。

def_en: a group of people (informal, often disapproving)
def_zh: 一帮人；一群人（非正式，常含贬义）
quote_en: A bunch of teenagers were hanging out at the mall.
quote_zh: 一帮青少年在商场里闲逛。
ex_en: This bunch were whispering excitedly, and Mr. Dursley saw no collecting tin.
ex_zh: 这帮人兴奋地窃窃私语，而德思礼先生没看到募捐罐。

def_en: a container used to collect money donations for a charity
quote_en: The volunteer shook the collecting tin at passing shoppers.
quote_zh: 志愿者向路过的购物者摇晃募捐罐。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley couldn't see a single collecting tin, so they weren't charity workers.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生一个募捐罐都没看到，所以他们不是慈善工作者。

def_en: holding something tightly, often with anxiety or eagerness
quote_en: The little girl was clutching her favorite doll.
quote_zh: 小女孩紧紧抓着她最喜欢的娃娃。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley walked back past them, clutching a large doughnut in a bag.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生走回去经过他们，手里紧握着一个装着大甜甜圈的袋子。

def_en: a sweet, fried ring of dough often covered with sugar or icing
quote_en: He bought a chocolate doughnut for breakfast.
quote_zh: 他买了一个巧克力甜甜圈当早餐。
ex_en: As Mr. Dursley clutched his doughnut, he overheard the word "Potters."
ex_zh: 当德思礼先生紧握着他的甜甜圈时，他无意中听到了"波特"这个词。

def_en: overheard part of a conversation; heard some of what was said
def_zh: 听到只言片语；无意中听到几句话
quote_en: I caught a few words of their conversation as I walked past.
quote_zh: 我走过时无意中听到了他们谈话的只言片语。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley caught a few words of what the people in cloaks were saying: "The Potters... their son, Harry."
ex_zh: 德思礼先生无意中听到了那些穿斗篷的人说的话："波特一家……他们的儿子，哈利。"

def_en: stopped suddenly and completely
quote_en: When he heard the crash, he stopped dead in his tracks.
quote_zh: 当他听到撞击声时，他突然停下了脚步。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley stopped dead when he heard the whisperers say "the Potters."
ex_zh: 当德思礼先生听到窃窃私语的人说"波特一家"时，他突然停下了脚步。

def_en: suddenly felt a strong wave of fear
def_zh: 恐惧涌上心头；被恐惧淹没
quote_en: Fear flooded him as he realized he was lost in the dark forest.
quote_zh: 当他意识到自己在黑暗的森林里迷路时，恐惧涌上心头。
ex_en: Fear flooded Mr. Dursley at the mention of the Potters.
ex_zh: 一提到波特一家，恐惧就涌上了德思礼先生的心头。

def_en: decided not to do something after thinking about it
def_zh: 深思熟虑后决定不做；改变了主意
quote_en: He almost complained, but thought better of it when he saw the boss's angry face.
quote_zh: 他差点抱怨，但看到老板愤怒的脸后决定不说了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley looked back as if to speak, but thought better of it.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生回头看好像要说什么，但想了想还是算了。

def_en: ran or moved quickly and suddenly
quote_en: She dashed across the street to catch the bus.
quote_zh: 她冲过马路去赶公交车。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley dashed back across the road and hurried up to his office.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生冲回马路对面，匆匆上楼回到办公室。

def_en: spoke to someone angrily and sharply
quote_en: The teacher snapped at the students for talking during the test.
quote_zh: 老师呵斥学生们在考试期间说话。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley snapped at his secretary not to disturb him.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生厉声告诉秘书不要打扰他。

def_en: interrupt someone when they are busy or resting
quote_en: Please do not disturb me while I am working.
quote_zh: 我工作时请不要打扰我。
ex_en: He snapped at his secretary not to disturb him while he made phone calls.
ex_zh: 他厉声告诉秘书在他打电话时不要打扰他。

def_en: took hold of something suddenly and forcefully
quote_en: He seized the opportunity to ask for a raise.
quote_zh: 他抓住机会要求加薪。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley seized his telephone and began to dial.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生抓起电话，开始拨号。

def_en: pressing buttons or turning a disc on a telephone to call someone
quote_en: She was dialing her mother's number when the doorbell rang.
quote_zh: 她正在拨妈妈的号码时门铃响了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley had almost finished dialing his home number when he changed his mind.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生几乎拨完了家里的号码，这时他改变了主意。

def_en: decided to do something different from what was originally planned
quote_en: I was going to buy the red dress, but I changed my mind and bought the blue one.
quote_zh: 我本来想买那条红裙子，但改变了主意买了蓝色的。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley changed his mind and put the receiver back down.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生改变了主意，把听筒放了回去。

def_en: the part of a telephone that you hold to your ear and mouth
quote_en: He picked up the receiver and dialed.
quote_zh: 他拿起听筒拨号。
ex_en: He put the receiver back down and stroked his mustache.
ex_zh: 他把听筒放回去，抚摸着他的胡子。

def_en: moved one's hand gently over something repeatedly
quote_en: She stroked the cat's soft fur.
quote_zh: 她抚摸着猫柔软的毛。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley stroked his mustache while thinking about the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生一边想着波特一家，一边抚摸着他的胡子。

def_en: used when you suddenly realize or remember something
quote_en: Come to think of it, I haven't seen him all day.
quote_zh: 说起来，我一整天都没见到他。
ex_en: Come to think of it, Mr. Dursley wasn't even sure his nephew was called Harry.
ex_zh: 说起来，德思礼先生甚至不确定他的外甥是不是叫哈利。

def_en: the son of one's sibling
quote_en: My nephew is coming to visit for the holidays.
quote_zh: 我的外甥要来度假。
ex_en: Harry Potter was Mr. Dursley's nephew, but he had never even seen him.
ex_zh: 哈利·波特是德思礼先生的外甥，但他从未见过他。

def_en: no reason or benefit to do something (there's no point in doing something)
quote_en: There's no point in crying over spilled milk.
quote_zh: 为打翻的牛奶哭泣是没有意义的。
ex_en: There was no point in worrying Mrs. Dursley; she always got upset about her sister.
ex_zh: 让德思礼夫人担心没有意义；她总是为妹妹的事心烦。

def_en: became very worried, angry, or distressed
quote_en: She gets so upset whenever anyone mentions the accident.
quote_zh: 每当有人提到那次事故，她就会变得非常心烦。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley always got so upset at any mention of her sister.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人一提到她妹妹就会变得非常心烦。

def_en: to hold someone responsible for something bad
quote_en: Don't blame me for the broken window; it wasn't my fault.
quote_zh: 别把打破窗户的责任推给我；那不是我的错。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley didn't blame his wife for being upset about her sister.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生不责怪妻子为妹妹的事心烦。

def_en: nevertheless; despite what has been said
quote_en: The weather was bad, but all the same, we went for a walk.
quote_zh: 天气不好，但尽管如此，我们还是去散步了。
ex_en: He tried to convince himself Potter was a common name, but all the same, those people in cloaks worried him.
ex_zh: 他试图说服自己波特是个普通姓氏，但尽管如此，那些穿斗篷的人还是让他担心。

def_en: to collide with someone or something while walking, without intending to
quote_en: He was looking at his phone and walked straight into a lamppost.
quote_zh: 他一边看手机一边走路，径直撞上了灯柱。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley was so worried that he walked straight into a tiny old man outside his office.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生太担心了，在办公室外径直撞上了一个矮小的老人。

def_en: made a short, low sound from the throat, often to show annoyance or effort
quote_en: He grunted a reply and continued eating.
quote_zh: 他咕哝着回答了一句，继续吃饭。
ex_en: "Sorry," grunted Mr. Dursley as the old man stumbled.
ex_zh: "抱歉，"德思礼先生咕哝着说，这时老人踉跄了一下。

def_en: tripped or lost balance while walking or moving
quote_en: She stumbled on the uneven pavement but didn't fall.
quote_zh: 她在不平的人行道上踉跄了一下，但没有摔倒。
ex_en: The tiny old man stumbled and almost fell after Mr. Dursley walked into him.
ex_zh: 那个矮小的老人被德思礼先生撞到后踉跄了一下，差点摔倒。

def_en: came very close to falling to the ground
quote_en: He slipped on the wet floor and almost fell.
quote_zh: 他在湿滑的地板上滑了一下，差点摔倒。
ex_en: The old man stumbled and almost fell, yet he was not upset at all.
ex_zh: 老人踉跄了一下差点摔倒，但他一点也不生气。

def_en: completely not; used to emphasize a negative statement
quote_en: She was not at all tired after the long walk.
quote_zh: 长途步行后她一点也不累。
ex_en: The old man was not at all upset at being almost knocked to the ground.
ex_zh: 那个老人差点被撞倒在地，却一点也不生气。

def_en: used to say that the opposite of a previous statement is true
quote_en: He didn't feel embarrassed; on the contrary, he was proud.
quote_zh: 他没有感到尴尬；相反，他很自豪。
ex_en: The old man wasn't upset; on the contrary, his face split into a wide smile.
ex_zh: 那个老人没有生气；相反，他的脸上绽开了灿烂的笑容。

def_en: suddenly formed a big, happy smile on one's face
def_zh: 绽开灿烂的笑容；咧嘴大笑
quote_en: When she heard the good news, her face split into a wide smile.
quote_zh: 当她听到好消息时，脸上绽开了灿烂的笑容。
ex_en: The old man's face split into a wide smile as he told Mr. Dursley to rejoice.
ex_zh: 老人的脸上绽开了灿烂的笑容，告诉德思礼先生要欢庆。

def_en: a voice that is high-pitched and often sounds like a squeak
def_zh: 尖细的声音；吱吱的声音
quote_en: The mouse‑like squeaky voice came from a tiny old lady.
quote_zh: 那老鼠般的尖细声音来自一位矮小的老妇人。
ex_en: The old man spoke in a squeaky voice that made passersby stare.
ex_zh: 那个老人用尖细的声音说话，让路人纷纷侧目。

def_en: people who are walking past a particular place
quote_en: The street performer attracted the attention of many passersby.
quote_zh: 街头艺人吸引了许多路人的注意。
ex_en: The old man's squeaky voice made passersby stare in surprise.
ex_zh: 老人尖细的声音让路人惊讶地注视。

def_en: put arms around someone and held them tightly, usually to show affection
quote_en: She hugged her grandmother tightly before leaving.
quote_zh: 她在离开前紧紧地拥抱了祖母。
ex_en: The old man hugged Mr. Dursley around the middle and then walked off.
ex_zh: 那个老人拦腰拥抱了德思礼先生，然后走开了。

def_en: a person one has never met before; a total unknown person
quote_en: He told his life story to a complete stranger on the train.
quote_zh: 他在火车上向一个完全陌生的人讲述了自己的一生。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley had been hugged by a complete stranger, which horrified him.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生被一个完全陌生的人拥抱了，这让他惊恐不已。

def_en: began a journey to return home
quote_en: After work, she set off for home through the busy streets.
quote_zh: 下班后，她穿过繁忙的街道动身回家。
ex_en: Rattled by everything, Mr. Dursley hurried to his car and set off for home.
ex_zh: 被这一切弄得慌乱不安，德思礼先生匆忙上车动身回家。

def_en: believing something that is not real or true; having false thoughts
quote_en: She thought she heard someone call her name, but she was just imagining things.
quote_zh: 她以为有人叫她名字，但那只是她在胡思乱想。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley hoped he was imagining things, though he had never hoped that before.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生希望自己是在胡思乱想，尽管他以前从未这样希望过。

def_en: had a negative opinion about something; thought something was wrong or bad (disapprove of)
quote_en: Her parents didn't approve of her staying out late.
quote_zh: 她的父母不赞成她在外面待到很晚。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley didn't approve of imagination, so hoping he was imagining things felt strange.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生不赞成胡思乱想，所以希望自己是在胡思乱想让他感觉很别扭。

def_en: to have a positive opinion about something; to think something is good or right
quote_en: I fully approve of your decision to study abroad.
quote_zh: 我完全赞成你出国留学的决定。
ex_en: The Dursleys did not approve of anything strange, mysterious, or magical.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家不赞成任何奇怪、神秘或魔法的事物。

def_en: drove into a private road leading to a house
quote_en: She pulled into the driveway and turned off the engine.
quote_zh: 她驶入车道，关掉了引擎。
ex_en: As Mr. Dursley pulled into the driveway of number four, the first thing he saw was the tabby cat.
ex_zh: 当德思礼先生驶入四号的车道时，他第一眼看到的就是那只虎斑猫。

def_en: make someone feel happier or less annoyed
quote_en: A cup of hot tea usually improves my mood.
quote_zh: 一杯热茶通常能改善我的心情。
ex_en: Seeing the cat again did not improve Mr. Dursley's mood.
ex_zh: 再次看到那只猫并没有改善德思礼先生的心情。

def_en: saw or noticed something, especially suddenly or with difficulty
quote_en: She spotted her friend in the crowded station.
quote_zh: 她在拥挤的车站发现了她的朋友。
ex_en: The tabby cat was the same one he had spotted that morning on the corner.
ex_zh: 那只虎斑猫就是他早上在拐角处发现的那只。

def_en: a low wall surrounding or bordering a garden
quote_en: The cat sat on the garden wall, watching the birds.
quote_zh: 猫坐在花园围墙上，看着鸟儿。
ex_en: The tabby cat was now sitting on the Dursleys' garden wall, refusing to move.
ex_zh: 那只虎斑猫现在正坐在德思礼家的花园围墙上，拒绝移动。

def_en: patterns or colors on an animal's fur or skin
quote_en: The leopard has distinctive dark markings on its golden fur.
quote_zh: 豹子金色的皮毛上有独特的深色斑纹。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley recognized the cat by the same markings around its eyes.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生通过眼睛周围的相同斑纹认出了那只猫。

def_en: a word used to drive away an animal or person
def_zh: 嘘（赶走动物或人的声音）
quote_en: "Shoo!" the farmer shouted at the chickens pecking his vegetables.
quote_zh: "嘘！"农夫对啄食他蔬菜的鸡喊道。
ex_en: "Shoo!" said Mr. Dursley loudly, but the cat didn't move.
ex_zh: "嘘！"德思礼先生大声说，但猫一动不动。

def_en: to calm down and control one's emotions after being upset
quote_en: After crying for a few minutes, she pulled herself together and continued.
quote_zh: 哭了几分钟后，她振作起来继续。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley tried to pull himself together and let himself into the house.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生努力振作起来，开门进了屋。

def_en: having made a firm decision not to do something
quote_en: She was determined not to let fear stop her.
quote_zh: 她决心不让恐惧阻止她。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley was still determined not to mention anything to his wife.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生仍然决心不向妻子提起任何事。

def_en: during the meal of dinner
quote_en: They discussed their plans over dinner.
quote_zh: 他们在晚餐时讨论了计划。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley told him all about the neighbors over dinner.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人在晚餐时把邻居的事都告诉了他。

def_en: to help a child get into bed and settle down for sleep
quote_en: The mother put her son to bed at eight o'clock.
quote_zh: 母亲八点把儿子安顿上床睡觉。
ex_en: When Dudley had been put to bed, Mr. Dursley went into the living room.
ex_zh: 达力被安顿上床睡觉后，德思礼先生走进了客厅。

def_en: early enough to see or hear something
quote_en: He arrived in time to catch the last train.
quote_zh: 他及时赶上了最后一班火车。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley went into the living room in time to catch the last report on the evening news.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生走进客厅，正好赶上晚间新闻的最后一条报道。

def_en: a television or radio news program broadcast in the early evening
quote_en: My father watches the evening news every day at six.
quote_zh: 我父亲每天六点看晚间新闻。
ex_en: The evening news reported strange owl behavior across the country.
ex_zh: 晚间新闻报道了全国范围内猫头鹰的奇怪行为。

def_en: people who observe and study wild birds as a hobby
quote_en: Bird-watchers gathered at the lake to see the rare swan.
quote_zh: 观鸟者聚集在湖边看那只罕见的天鹅。
ex_en: Bird-watchers everywhere reported that owls were behaving very unusually.
ex_zh: 各地的观鸟者都报告说猫头鹰的行为非常反常。

def_en: acting in a way that is not normal or typical
quote_en: The dog was behaving unusually, barking at nothing.
quote_zh: 那只狗行为反常，对着空气叫。
ex_en: Owls were behaving unusually, flying in daylight instead of hunting at night.
ex_zh: 猫头鹰行为反常，在大白天飞行而不是夜间捕食。

def_en: occasions when something is seen, especially something rare or unusual
quote_en: There have been several sightings of the rare bird this week.
quote_zh: 本周已有几次目击到这种稀有鸟类。
ex_en: There have been hundreds of sightings of owls flying in every direction since sunrise.
ex_zh: 自日出以来，已有数百次目击到猫头鹰朝各个方向飞行。

def_en: the regular habit or schedule of sleeping
quote_en: Working night shifts can disrupt your sleeping pattern.
quote_zh: 上夜班会扰乱你的睡眠模式。
ex_en: Experts are unable to explain why the owls have suddenly changed their sleeping pattern.
ex_zh: 专家无法解释猫头鹰为何突然改变了睡眠模式。

def_en: permitted himself to smile slightly, often with amusement
quote_en: The teacher allowed himself a grin at the student's clever joke.
quote_zh: 老师听到学生机智的笑话忍不住微微一笑。
ex_en: The newscaster allowed himself a grin and said, "Most mysterious."
ex_zh: 新闻播音员忍不住微微一笑说："最神秘了。"

def_en: very strange and difficult to understand (used for emphasis)
quote_en: The disappearance of the keys was most mysterious.
quote_zh: 钥匙的失踪最是神秘。
ex_en: The newscaster described the owl behavior as "most mysterious."
ex_zh: 新闻播音员称猫头鹰的行为"最是神秘"。

def_en: a large number of owls appearing like rain (metaphorical)
def_zh: 一阵阵的猫头鹰；猫头鹰雨
quote_en: The weatherman joked about showers of owls instead of rain.
quote_zh: 天气预报员开玩笑说会有猫头鹰雨而不是下雨。
ex_en: "Going to be any more showers of owls tonight, Jim?" asked the newscaster.
ex_zh: "今晚还会有猫头鹰雨吗，吉姆？"新闻播音员问道。

def_en: behaving in a strange or unusual way
quote_en: The computer started acting oddly, freezing every few minutes.
quote_zh: 电脑开始行为古怪，每隔几分钟就死机。
ex_en: It's not only the owls that have been acting oddly today, said the weatherman.
ex_zh: 天气预报员说，今天行为古怪的不只是猫头鹰。

def_en: calling a radio or television program by telephone to give information
quote_en: Listeners have been phoning in to share their stories.
quote_zh: 听众纷纷打电话来分享他们的故事。
ex_en: Viewers from Kent, Yorkshire, and Dundee have been phoning in to report shooting stars.
ex_zh: 来自肯特、约克郡和邓迪的观众纷纷打来电话报告流星。

def_en: a very heavy, sudden fall of meteors (like heavy rain)
quote_en: Instead of rain, they had a downpour of shooting stars.
quote_zh: 他们遇到的不是雨，而是一场流星暴雨。
ex_en: Viewers reported a downpour of shooting stars instead of the promised rain.
ex_zh: 观众报告说遇到的不是预报的雨，而是一场流星暴雨。

def_en: a British celebration on November 5th with bonfires and fireworks
def_zh: 篝火之夜（英国11月5日的节日）
quote_en: Bonfire Night commemorates the failure of the Gunpowder Plot.
quote_zh: 篝火之夜是为了纪念火药阴谋的失败。
ex_en: The weatherman joked that people might be celebrating Bonfire Night early.
ex_zh: 天气预报员开玩笑说人们可能是在提前庆祝篝火之夜。

def_en: a night when it rains
quote_en: We stayed indoors because it was a wet night.
quote_zh: 因为是个雨夜，我们待在室内。
ex_en: The weatherman promised a wet night tonight with rain.
ex_zh: 天气预报员承诺今晚会是一个有雨的雨夜。

def_en: remained completely still and unmoving, usually from fear or shock
def_zh: 呆坐着；一动不动地坐着
quote_en: She sat frozen in her seat when she heard the strange noise.
quote_zh: 她听到奇怪的声响时呆坐在座位上一动不动。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley sat frozen in his armchair as he connected the strange events to the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生呆坐在扶手椅上，把这些奇怪的事件和波特一家联系起来。

def_en: everywhere; in many different locations
quote_en: After the party, there were empty cups all over the place.
quote_zh: 派对结束后，到处是空杯子。
ex_en: There were mysterious people in cloaks all over the place, and owls flying by daylight.
ex_zh: 到处是穿着斗篷的神秘人，还有猫头鹰在大白天飞行。

def_en: coughed slightly to make one's voice clear, often before speaking
quote_en: The speaker cleared his throat and began his speech.
quote_zh: 演讲者清了清嗓子，开始演讲。
ex_en: He cleared his throat nervously before asking about her sister.
ex_zh: 他紧张地清了清嗓子，然后问起她妹妹的事。

def_en: received news or a message from someone (usually by letter, phone, etc.)
quote_en: I haven't heard from my cousin since she moved to Canada.
quote_zh: 自从我表妹搬到加拿大后，我就没收到过她的消息。
ex_en: "You haven't heard from your sister lately, have you?" Mr. Dursley asked.
ex_zh: "你最近没有收到你妹妹的消息吧？"德思礼先生问道。

def_en: recently; in the recent past
quote_en: I've been feeling tired lately.
quote_zh: 我最近一直觉得很累。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley asked Petunia if she had heard from her sister lately.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生问佩妮最近是否收到了她妹妹的消息。

def_en: strange or odd in appearance
def_zh: 长相奇怪的；样子古怪的
quote_en: He wore a funny-looking hat that made everyone laugh.
quote_zh: 他戴了一顶样子古怪的帽子，逗得大家哈哈大笑。
ex_en: There were a lot of funny-looking people in town today, Mr. Dursley said.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生说，今天镇上有许多长相古怪的人。

def_en: the group of people associated with someone, often with disapproval
quote_en: She didn't want her children mixing with his crowd.
quote_zh: 她不想让她的孩子们和他那伙人混在一起。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley wondered if the strange events had something to do with "her crowd" – the Potters' magical world.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生猜想这些奇怪的事件是否与"她那一类人"——波特家的魔法世界——有关。

def_en: in a way that appears relaxed and unconcerned, despite inner feelings
def_zh: 尽量随意地；装作漫不经心地
quote_en: He asked about her ex-boyfriend as casually as he could.
quote_zh: 他尽量随意地问起她的前男友。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley asked about the Potters' son as casually as he could, not wanting to alarm his wife.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生尽量随意地问起波特家的儿子，不想引起妻子的警觉。

def_en: unpleasant, unkind, or of poor quality (nasty person, nasty name, nasty smell)
def_zh: 令人讨厌的；刻薄的；难听的（讨厌的人，难听的名字，难闻的气味）
quote_en: That was a nasty thing to say to your friend.
quote_zh: 对你的朋友说那种话真刻薄。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley called Harry a "nasty, common name" without ever having met the boy.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人称哈利是个"难听的、普通的名字"，尽管她从未见过那个男孩。

def_en: a name that is ordinary, frequently used, and not special
def_zh: 普通的名字；常见的名字
quote_en: John is such a common name that there are three in my class.
quote_zh: 约翰是个非常普通的名字，我班上有三个。
ex_en: Mrs. Dursley sneered that Harry was a nasty, common name.
ex_zh: 德思礼夫人讥讽说哈利是个难听的、普通的名字。

def_en: a feeling of sudden disappointment or dread
quote_en: His heart sank when he saw the test score.
quote_zh: 看到考试成绩时，他的心一沉。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley's heart sank horribly when he heard the name "Harry."
ex_zh: 当德思礼先生听到"哈利"这个名字时，他的心可怕地一沉。

def_en: looked carefully or with difficulty downward
quote_en: She peered down into the dark basement, afraid of what she might see.
quote_zh: 她向下凝视黑暗的地下室，害怕会看到什么。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley crept to the bedroom window and peered down into the front garden.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生蹑手蹑脚地走到卧室窗户边，向下凝视前花园。

def_en: as if; in a way that suggests something is true
quote_en: He looked at me as though he had never seen me before.
quote_zh: 他看着我的样子好像从未见过我。
ex_en: The cat was staring down Privet Drive as though it were waiting for something.
ex_zh: 那只猫凝视着女贞路，仿佛在等待着什么。

def_en: (of information) became known to others, especially when meant to be secret
quote_en: If this secret gets out, we'll be in trouble.
quote_zh: 如果这个秘密泄露出去，我们就麻烦了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley feared what would happen if it got out that they were related to the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生担心如果他们是波特家亲戚这件事泄露出去会怎样。

def_en: belonging to the same family; connected by blood or marriage
quote_en: She is related to the famous singer on her mother's side.
quote_zh: 她母亲那边和那位著名歌手有亲戚关系。
ex_en: The Dursleys were related to the Potters, but they pretended otherwise.
ex_zh: 德思礼一家与波特家有亲属关系，但他们假装没有。

word: turning it all over in his mind
def_en: thinking carefully and repeatedly about something
def_zh: 在脑子里反复思考；仔细琢磨
quote_en: He lay awake, turning the problem over in his mind.
quote_zh: 他醒着躺在床上，在脑子里反复思考那个问题。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley lay awake, turning it all over in his mind – the owls, the cloaks, the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生醒着躺在床上，反复琢磨着这一切——猫头鹰、斗篷、波特一家。

def_en: a thought that makes one feel less worried or sad
quote_en: The comforting thought that tomorrow would be better helped her fall asleep.
quote_zh: 明天会更好的宽慰想法帮助她入睡了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley's last comforting thought was that the Potters had no reason to come near them.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生最后一个宽慰的想法是：波特一家没有理由靠近他们。

def_en: become involved in something unpleasant or troublesome
quote_en: He didn't want to get mixed up in their argument.
quote_zh: 他不想卷入他们的争吵。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley couldn't see how he and Petunia could get mixed up in anything that was going on.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生看不出他和佩妮怎么会卷入任何正在发生的事情中。

def_en: opened one's mouth wide and breathed in deeply, usually when tired or bored
quote_en: The student yawned during the long lecture.
quote_zh: 那个学生在冗长的讲座中打了个哈欠。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley yawned and turned over, convinced that nothing could affect them.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生打了个哈欠翻过身，确信什么都不会影响到他们。

def_en: to influence or cause a change in someone or something
quote_en: The weather can greatly affect your mood.
quote_zh: 天气会极大地影响你的心情。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley told himself that whatever was happening couldn't affect him and his family.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生告诉自己，无论发生什么都不会影响到他和他的家人。

def_en: gradually falling into a state or sleep
quote_en: She was drifting into a pleasant dream when the phone rang.
quote_zh: 她正渐渐进入美梦时电话响了。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley might have been drifting into an uneasy sleep, but the cat was wide awake.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生也许正渐渐进入不安的睡眠，但那只猫却很清醒。

def_en: a restless sleep disturbed by worry or discomfort
quote_en: He had an uneasy sleep after watching a horror movie.
quote_zh: 看完恐怖片后他睡得不安稳。
ex_en: Mr. Dursley drifted into an uneasy sleep, troubled by thoughts of the Potters.
ex_zh: 德思礼先生渐渐进入不安的睡眠，被对波特一家的担忧所困扰。

def_en: not displaying any indication or evidence of something
def_zh: 毫无……的迹象；没有任何……的表现
quote_en: The patient was showing no sign of improvement.
quote_zh: 病人没有任何好转的迹象。
ex_en: The cat was showing no sign of sleepiness, sitting as still as a statue.
ex_zh: 那只猫毫无睡意，像雕像一样一动不动地坐着。

def_en: completely motionless, like a stone statue
quote_en: The guard stood as still as a statue for hours.
quote_zh: 那个卫兵像雕像一样一动不动地站了几个小时。
ex_en: The cat sat on the wall as still as a statue, its eyes fixed on the corner.
ex_zh: 猫像雕像一样一动不动地坐在墙上，眼睛盯着拐角处。

def_en: staring steadily without blinking
def_zh: 目不转睛地；凝视不动地
quote_en: The owl fixed unblinkingly on its prey.
quote_zh: 猫头鹰目不转睛地盯着猎物。
ex_en: The cat's eyes were fixed unblinkingly on the far corner of Privet Drive.
ex_zh: 猫的眼睛目不转睛地盯着女贞路远处的拐角。

def_en: did not move or shake even a tiny amount
def_zh: 甚至一动不动；丝毫未颤抖
quote_en: The soldier didn't so much as flinch when the explosion occurred.
quote_zh: 爆炸发生时，那个士兵甚至没有退缩一下。
ex_en: The cat didn't so much as quiver when a car door slammed nearby.
ex_zh: 附近有车门砰地关上时，那只猫甚至一动不动。

def_en: shut or closed with great force, making a loud noise
quote_en: The wind slammed the door shut.
quote_zh: 风把门砰地关上了。
ex_en: A car door slammed on the next street, but the cat didn't react.
ex_zh: 隔壁街上有人砰地关上车门，但那只猫毫无反应。

def_en: appeared suddenly from below the ground, as if emerging from underground
quote_en: The rabbit popped out of the ground and ran away.
quote_zh: 兔子突然从地下冒出来跑掉了。
ex_en: The man appeared so suddenly that you'd have thought he'd just popped out of the ground.
ex_zh: 那个男人出现得如此突然，你会以为他刚从地下冒出来。

def_en: moved suddenly and slightly, often involuntarily
quote_en: The dog's nose twitched as it smelled the food.
quote_zh: 狗的鼻子闻到食物时抽动了一下。
ex_en: The cat's tail twitched and its eyes narrowed as the man appeared.
ex_zh: 那个男人出现时，猫的尾巴抽动了一下，眼睛也眯了起来。

def_en: became partly closed, as when focusing or showing suspicion
quote_en: She narrowed her eyes to read the small print.
quote_zh: 她眯起眼睛看小字。
ex_en: The cat's eyes narrowed as it watched the strange man.
ex_zh: 猫眯起眼睛看着那个陌生的男人。

def_en: forming an opinion based on something
quote_en: Judging by his accent, he is from the south.
quote_zh: 从他的口音来判断，他来自南方。
ex_en: The man was very old, judging by the silver of his hair and beard.
ex_zh: 从他那银白色的头发和胡子来看，那个男人非常老了。

def_en: put the end of something under or inside one's belt to hold it
quote_en: He tucked his long shirt into his belt.
quote_zh: 他把长衬衫的下摆塞进腰带里。
ex_en: Dumbledore's beard was long enough to tuck into his belt.
ex_zh: 邓布利多的胡子长得可以塞进腰带里。

def_en: long, loose outer garments, often worn as official or ceremonial clothing
quote_en: The wizards wore flowing robes.
quote_zh: 巫师们穿着飘逸的长袍。
ex_en: Dumbledore was wearing long robes and a purple cloak.
ex_zh: 邓布利多穿着长袍和紫色斗篷。

def_en: touched or brushed along the ground as it hung
quote_en: Her wedding dress swept the ground as she walked.
quote_zh: 她的婚纱拖在地上。
ex_en: Dumbledore's purple cloak swept the ground as he walked.
ex_zh: 邓布利多的紫色斗篷拖在地上。

def_en: boots with raised heels and straps with buckles
quote_en: He wore old-fashioned high-heeled, buckled boots.
quote_zh: 他穿着老式的高跟扣带靴。
ex_en: Dumbledore's high-heeled, buckled boots were unlike anything on Privet Drive.
ex_zh: 邓布利多的高跟扣带靴在女贞路上前所未见。

def_en: shining brightly with small flashes of light
quote_en: Her sparkling eyes showed her excitement.
quote_zh: 她闪闪发光的眼睛显示出她的兴奋。
ex_en: Dumbledore's blue eyes were light, bright, and sparkling behind half-moon spectacles.
ex_zh: 邓布利多的蓝眼睛在半月形眼镜后面明亮而闪闪发光。

def_en: glasses with lenses shaped like a half-circle
quote_en: The old professor wore half-moon spectacles low on his nose.
quote_zh: 老教授戴着半月形眼镜，架在鼻梁下方。
ex_en: Dumbledore's half-moon spectacles made him look wise and kind.
ex_zh: 邓布利多的半月形眼镜让他看起来睿智而和善。

def_en: bent or twisted, not straight
quote_en: The old tree had a crooked trunk.
quote_zh: 那棵老树的树干是弯曲的。
ex_en: Dumbledore's nose was very long and crooked, as though it had been broken at least twice.
ex_zh: 邓布利多的鼻子又长又弯，好像至少断过两次。

def_en: searching inside something in a disorganized or hurried way
quote_en: She was rummaging through her bag for her keys.
quote_zh: 她在包里翻找钥匙。
ex_en: Dumbledore was busy rummaging in his cloak, looking for something.
ex_zh: 邓布利多正忙着在他的斗篷里翻找东西。

def_en: cause him to find something funny or entertaining
quote_en: The kitten's silly behavior amused him.
quote_zh: 小猫的傻乎乎的行为逗乐了他。
ex_en: The sight of the cat seemed to amuse Dumbledore.
ex_zh: 那只猫的样子似乎让邓布利多觉得好笑。

def_en: laughed quietly or to oneself
quote_en: He chuckled at the memory of the joke.
quote_zh: 想起那个笑话，他轻声笑了。
ex_en: Dumbledore chuckled and muttered, "I should have known."
ex_zh: 邓布利多轻声笑了，咕哝道："我早该知道。"

def_en: spoke quietly and unclearly, often to oneself
quote_en: He muttered something under his breath and walked away.
quote_zh: 他低声说了些什么，然后走开了。
ex_en: Dumbledore muttered to himself as he looked at the cat.
ex_zh: 邓布利多看着那只猫，喃喃自语。

def_en: a small device that produces a flame for lighting cigarettes
quote_en: He flicked his cigarette lighter to light the candle.
quote_zh: 他啪地打开打火机点燃蜡烛。
ex_en: What Dumbledore pulled from his pocket seemed to be a silver cigarette lighter.
ex_zh: 邓布利多从口袋里掏出的东西看起来像是一个银色打火机。

def_en: opened a device with a quick, sharp movement of the finger
quote_en: He flicked the lighter open and lit the fire.
quote_zh: 他啪地打开打火机点燃了火。
ex_en: Dumbledore flicked the Put-Outer open and held it up in the air.
ex_zh: 邓布利多啪地打开熄灯器，把它举在空中。

def_en: pressed or operated a device to produce a short, sharp sound
quote_en: He clicked his pen and started writing.
quote_zh: 他咔嗒一声按开笔，开始写字。
ex_en: Dumbledore clicked the Put-Outer, and the nearest street lamp went out.
ex_zh: 邓布利多咔嗒一声按下熄灯器，最近的那盏路灯就熄灭了。

word: went out with a little pop
def_en: extinguished suddenly, making a small popping sound
quote_en: The candle went out with a little pop when the wind blew.
quote_zh: 风吹来时，蜡烛啪的一声熄灭了。
ex_en: The nearest street lamp went out with a little pop when Dumbledore clicked the Put-Outer.
ex_zh: 邓布利多按下熄灯器时，最近的那盏路灯啪的一声熄灭了。

def_en: gradually went out with a flickering light before becoming dark
quote_en: The old bulb flickered into darkness after a few seconds.
quote_zh: 旧灯泡闪烁了几下后熄灭了。
ex_en: Dumbledore clicked again, and the next lamp flickered into darkness.
ex_zh: 邓布利多又按了一下，下一盏灯闪烁着熄灭了。

def_en: tiny, sharp points of light (like the marks made by pins)
quote_en: In the distance, the city lights looked like pinpricks of light.
quote_zh: 远处的城市灯光看起来像针尖般的光点。
ex_en: The only lights left on the whole street were two tiny pinpricks — the cat's eyes.
ex_zh: 整条街上只剩下两个针尖般的光点——那是猫的眼睛。

def_en: having small, bright, and watchful eyes (often suggesting suspicion or greed)
def_zh: 眼睛小而亮的；目光锐利的
quote_en: The beady-eyed bird watched the worm from a branch.
quote_zh: 那只眼睛小而亮的鸟在树枝上盯着虫子。
ex_en: Even beady-eyed Mrs. Dursley wouldn't be able to see anything happening on the pavement.
ex_zh: 即使是眼睛锐利的德思礼夫人也看不见人行道上发生的一切。

def_en: a paved path for pedestrians beside a road
quote_en: She walked along the pavement, window shopping.
quote_zh: 她沿着人行道边走边看橱窗。
ex_en: If anyone looked out now, they wouldn't see what was happening on the pavement.
ex_zh: 如果现在有人往外看，他们看不见人行道上正在发生什么。

def_en: put something back into a place quietly or quickly
quote_en: He slipped the letter back into his pocket.
quote_zh: 他把信悄悄放回口袋。
ex_en: Dumbledore slipped the Put-Outer back inside his cloak.
ex_zh: 邓布利多把熄灯器塞回斗篷里。

def_en: began walking along the street
quote_en: After breakfast, he set off down the street to work.
quote_zh: 早餐后，他沿着街道走去上班。
ex_en: Dumbledore set off down the street toward number four, Privet Drive.
ex_zh: 邓布利多沿着街道向女贞路四号走去。

def_en: having a strict, serious, or harsh appearance
def_zh: 样子严厉的；面容严肃的
quote_en: The severe-looking librarian shushed everyone who spoke.
quote_zh: 那位样子严厉的图书馆管理员让每个说话的人都安静。
ex_en: Instead of the cat, Dumbledore was smiling at a rather severe-looking woman.
ex_zh: 邓布利多面对的不再是猫，而是一个样子相当严厉的女人。

def_en: eyeglasses with frames that are square in shape
quote_en: He wore square glasses that made him look intellectual.
quote_zh: 他戴着方形眼镜，看起来很知识分子。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall wore square glasses exactly the shape of the cat's markings.
ex_zh: 麦格教授戴着方形眼镜，形状与那只猫的斑纹一模一样。

def_en: (of hair) pulled back and twisted into a round, compact shape at the back of the head
quote_en: Her long hair was drawn into a tight bun at the nape of her neck.
quote_zh: 她的长发在颈后梳成紧紧的发髻。
ex_en: McGonagall's black hair was drawn into a tight bun, giving her a strict appearance.
ex_zh: 麦格教授的黑发梳成紧紧的发髻，让她看起来十分严厉。

def_en: clearly annoyed, upset, or disarranged (both emotionally and literally)
def_zh: 明显心烦意乱的；有些恼火的
quote_en: She looked distinctly ruffled after the argument.
quote_zh: 争吵之后她明显心烦意乱。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall looked distinctly ruffled, both from sitting all day and from Dumbledore's teasing.
ex_zh: 麦格教授明显有些恼火，既因为坐了一整天，也因为邓布利多的调侃。

def_en: made a short, audible breath through the nose to show annoyance
def_zh: 生气地哼了一声；气愤地吸鼻子
quote_en: When he heard the excuse, he sniffed angrily and walked away.
quote_zh: 听到那个借口，他生气地哼了一声走开了。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall sniffed angrily at Dumbledore's suggestion that she should have been celebrating.
ex_zh: 麦格教授对邓布利多说她本该去庆祝的建议生气地哼了一声。

def_en: in a way that shows annoyance at having to wait or deal with something
quote_en: "Hurry up," she said impatiently.
quote_zh: "快点，"她不耐烦地说。
ex_en: "Oh yes, everyone's celebrating, all right," she said impatiently.
ex_zh: "哦，是的，大家都在庆祝，行了吧，"她不耐烦地说。

def_en: to panic or act irrationally
quote_en: Stay calm and don't lose your heads – we can solve this.
quote_zh: 保持冷静，不要慌乱 – 我们能解决这个问题。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall warned that people were losing their heads instead of being careful.
ex_zh: 麦格教授警告说人们不是在小心行事，而是在失去理智。

def_en: completely or extremely (used to emphasize a negative quality)
quote_en: That's downright dangerous!
quote_zh: 那简直太危险了！
ex_en: People are being downright careless, out on the streets in broad daylight.
ex_zh: 人们简直太粗心了，大白天跑到街上去。

def_en: exchanging pieces of unverified information or gossip
quote_en: The children were swapping rumors about the haunted house.
quote_zh: 孩子们在互相传着关于鬼屋的谣言。
ex_en: People in cloaks were swapping rumors instead of being discreet.
ex_zh: 穿斗篷的人们在交换谣言，而不是保持谨慎。

def_en: a quick, piercing look directed to the side, often expressing suspicion or a desire for information
quote_en: She gave him a sharp, sideways glance, waiting for an answer.
quote_zh: 她侧目锐视了他一眼，等待回答。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall threw a sharp, sideways glance at Dumbledore, hoping he would tell her something.
ex_zh: 麦格教授侧目锐视了邓布利多一眼，希望他能告诉她些什么。

def_en: a type of hard, lemon-flavored candy
quote_en: He offered her a lemon drop from his pocket.
quote_zh: 他从口袋里拿出一颗柠檬雪宝给她。
ex_en: Dumbledore was rather fond of Muggle sweets, especially lemon drops.
ex_zh: 邓布利多很喜欢麻瓜糖果，尤其是柠檬雪宝。

def_en: liking something quite a bit; having a mild affection for
quote_en: She's rather fond of classical music.
quote_zh: 她相当喜欢古典音乐。
ex_en: Dumbledore explained that lemon drops were a kind of Muggle sweet he was rather fond of.
ex_zh: 邓布利多解释说柠檬雪宝是一种他相当喜欢的麻瓜糖果。

def_en: a person who shows good judgment and practicality
def_zh: 明智的人；通情达理的人
quote_en: Any sensible person would bring an umbrella on a cloudy day.
quote_zh: 任何明智的人都会在阴天带伞。
ex_en: Dumbledore suggested that a sensible person like McGonagall could call Voldemort by his name.
ex_zh: 邓布利多说像麦格这样明智的人可以用真名称呼伏地魔。

def_en: to cause someone to do or believe something through reasoning or argument
quote_en: She persuaded her friend to join the club.
quote_zh: 她说服朋友加入了俱乐部。
ex_en: For eleven years Dumbledore had been trying to persuade people to say Voldemort's name.
ex_zh: 十一年来邓布利多一直试图说服人们说出伏地魔的名字。

def_en: the real or official name of a person or thing
quote_en: His proper name is James, but everyone calls him Jim.
quote_zh: 他的正式名字是詹姆斯，但大家都叫他吉姆。
ex_en: Dumbledore wanted people to use Voldemort's proper name instead of "You-Know-Who."
ex_zh: 邓布利多希望人们使用伏地魔的真名而不是"神秘人"。

def_en: made a sudden, involuntary movement in reaction to fear or pain
quote_en: He flinched when the needle went in.
quote_zh: 针扎进去时他畏缩了一下。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall flinched when Dumbledore said the name Voldemort.
ex_zh: 邓布利多说"伏地魔"这个名字时，麦格教授畏缩了一下。

def_en: separating two things that are stuck together
quote_en: He was unsticking two stamps that had been stuck together.
quote_zh: 他在分开两张粘在一起的邮票。
ex_en: Dumbledore was unsticking two lemon drops while McGonagall flinched at Voldemort's name.
ex_zh: 邓布利多正在分开两颗粘在一起的柠檬雪宝，而麦格教授听到伏地魔的名字时畏缩了一下。

def_en: very annoyed or frustrated
quote_en: After the third explanation, the teacher was exasperated.
quote_zh: 解释了三次之后，老师恼怒不已。
ex_en: McGonagall sounded half exasperated, half admiring when she spoke to Dumbledore.
ex_zh: 麦格对邓布利多说话时半是恼怒半是钦佩。

def_en: showing respect or approval for someone's qualities
quote_en: She gave him an admiring look.
quote_zh: 她向他投去赞赏的目光。
ex_en: McGonagall admitted that Voldemort had been afraid of Dumbledore, her tone half admiring.
ex_zh: 麦格承认伏地魔害怕邓布利多，语气中半是钦佩。

def_en: became red in the face from embarrassment or shyness
quote_en: She blushed when he complimented her dress.
quote_zh: 当他称赞她的裙子时，她脸红了。
ex_en: Dumbledore joked that he hadn't blushed so much since Madam Pomfrey liked his new earmuffs.
ex_zh: 邓布利多开玩笑说自从庞弗雷夫人喜欢他的新耳罩后，他还没这么脸红过。

def_en: a pair of padded coverings worn over the ears to keep them warm
quote_en: He wore earmuffs to protect his ears from the cold wind.
quote_zh: 他戴着耳罩保护耳朵免受寒风。
ex_en: Dumbledore mentioned Madam Pomfrey liking his new earmuffs as an example of a time he blushed.
ex_zh: 邓布利多提到庞弗雷夫人喜欢他的新耳罩，作为他曾经脸红的一个例子。

def_en: gave a quick, piercing glance, often to convey a message or warning
quote_en: The teacher shot a sharp look at the whispering students.
quote_zh: 老师向窃窃私语的学生投去锐利的一瞥。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall shot a sharp look at Dumbledore before continuing.
ex_zh: 麦格教授向邓布利多投去锐利的一瞥，然后继续说。

def_en: compared with; in contrast to
quote_en: Her problems are nothing next to his.
quote_zh: 她的问题与他的相比不算什么。
ex_en: "The owls are nothing next to the rumors that are flying around," said McGonagall.
ex_zh: "猫头鹰与四处流传的谣言相比不算什么，"麦格说。

def_en: (of information or rumors) being spread quickly among people
quote_en: Wild rumors were flying around the school.
quote_zh: 学校里四处流传着离谱的谣言。
ex_en: Rumors about why Voldemort had disappeared were flying around everywhere.
ex_zh: 关于伏地魔为何消失的谣言四处流传。

def_en: arrived at the most important or crucial part of a discussion
def_zh: 说到点子上；到了关键时刻
quote_en: After a long introduction, he finally reached the point of his speech.
quote_zh: 经过长长的开场白，他终于说到了演讲的要点。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall had finally reached the point she was most anxious to discuss.
ex_zh: 麦格教授终于说到了她最急于讨论的要点。

def_en: eager or worried about talking about something
def_zh: 急于讨论的；迫切想谈的
quote_en: She was anxious to discuss her plans before it was too late.
quote_zh: 她急于讨论她的计划，以免为时已晚。
ex_en: McGonagall had been waiting all day to reach the topic she was most anxious to discuss.
ex_zh: 麦格等了一整天，就是为了说到她最急于讨论的话题。

def_en: a very intense, penetrating look
def_zh: 锐利的凝视；洞穿的目光
quote_en: The detective gave the suspect a piercing stare.
quote_zh: 侦探向嫌疑人投去锐利的凝视。
ex_en: Neither as a cat nor as a woman had she fixed Dumbledore with such a piercing stare.
ex_zh: 无论是作为猫还是作为女人，她都从未用如此锐利的目光凝视过邓布利多。

def_en: obvious or clear
quote_en: It was plain that she was unhappy with the decision.
quote_zh: 很明显她对那个决定不满意。
ex_en: It was plain that McGonagall wouldn't believe anything until Dumbledore told her it was true.
ex_zh: 很明显，除非邓布利多告诉她那是真的，否则麦格什么都不会相信。

def_en: selecting another piece of lemon candy, deliberately avoiding answering
quote_en: While others waited for an answer, he occupied himself with choosing another candy.
quote_zh: 当别人等待答案时，他自顾自地又选了一颗糖。
ex_en: Dumbledore, however, was choosing another lemon drop and did not answer.
ex_zh: 然而邓布利多却又选了一颗柠檬雪宝，没有回答。

def_en: continued speaking or moving forward despite difficulty or hesitation
quote_en: Despite the interruption, she pressed on with her story.
quote_zh: 尽管被打断，她还是坚持讲完了她的故事。
ex_en: "What they're saying," McGonagall pressed on, "is that Voldemort turned up in Godric's Hollow."
ex_zh: "他们说的是，"麦格坚持说，"伏地魔出现在戈德里克山谷。"

def_en: appeared or arrived, often unexpectedly
quote_en: He turned up late to the meeting without any explanation.
quote_zh: 他没有任何解释地开会迟到了。
ex_en: Voldemort turned up in Godric's Hollow last night to find the Potters.
ex_zh: 伏地魔昨晚出现在戈德里克山谷去找波特一家。

def_en: lowered his head forward, often as a sign of grief, respect, or shame
quote_en: He bowed his head in silence when he heard the sad news.
quote_zh: 听到悲伤的消息时，他默默地低下了头。
ex_en: Dumbledore bowed his head when McGonagall told him the Potters were dead.
ex_zh: 当麦格告诉邓布利多波特夫妇死了时，邓布利多低下了头。

def_en: took a sudden, sharp breath in surprise or shock
def_zh: 倒吸一口气；吃惊地喘息
quote_en: She gasped when she saw the price tag.
quote_zh: 看到价格标签时她倒吸了一口气。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall gasped when Dumbledore confirmed that Lily and James were dead.
ex_zh: 当邓布利多确认莉莉和詹姆死了时，麦格教授倒吸了一口气。

def_en: extended one's arm forward, often to touch or comfort someone
quote_en: He reached out and took her hand.
quote_zh: 他伸出手握住了她的手。
ex_en: Dumbledore reached out and patted her on the shoulder.
ex_zh: 邓布利多伸出手拍了拍她的肩膀。

def_en: touched gently and repeatedly with the flat hand, usually to comfort
quote_en: She patted the dog's head affectionately.
quote_zh: 她疼爱地轻拍狗的头。
ex_en: Dumbledore patted McGonagall on the shoulder to comfort her.
ex_zh: 邓布利多轻拍麦格的肩膀安慰她。

def_en: in a way that shows sadness, tiredness, or difficulty
quote_en: He sighed heavily and walked away.
quote_zh: 他沉重地叹了口气走开了。
ex_en: "I know... I know..." said Dumbledore heavily.
ex_zh: "我知道……我知道……"邓布利多沉重地说。

def_en: spoke with an unsteady voice, often from strong emotion
quote_en: Her voice trembled as she told the story.
quote_zh: 她讲故事时声音颤抖。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall's voice trembled as she went on about Harry.
ex_zh: 麦格教授继续讲哈利的事情时声音颤抖。

def_en: magical ability suddenly failed or was destroyed
def_zh: 力量莫名其妙地崩溃了
quote_en: The spell was too strong, and his power broke.
quote_zh: 咒语太强了，他的力量崩溃了。
ex_en: When Voldemort couldn't kill Harry, his power somehow broke.
ex_zh: 当伏地魔杀不死哈利时，他的力量莫名其妙地崩溃了。

def_en: moved his head up and down in a sad, depressed manner
def_zh: 阴郁地点点头；闷闷不乐地点头
quote_en: He nodded glumly when he heard the bad news.
quote_zh: 听到坏消息时他阴郁地点了点头。
ex_en: Dumbledore nodded glumly, confirming that the rumors were true.
ex_zh: 邓布利多阴郁地点点头，确认那些谣言是真的。

def_en: spoke in a hesitant or unsteady way, often from doubt or fear
quote_en: "I... I don't know," she faltered.
quote_zh: "我……我不知道，"她结巴地说。
ex_en: "It's – it's true?" faltered Professor McGonagall.
ex_zh: "这——这是真的吗？"麦格教授结巴地问。

def_en: so surprising that it is hard to believe
quote_en: The magician's final trick was astounding.
quote_zh: 魔术师的最后一个戏法令人震惊。
ex_en: It's just astounding that Voldemort couldn't kill a little boy.
ex_zh: 伏地魔杀不死一个小男孩，这太令人震惊了。

def_en: to continue to live after a dangerous event or illness
quote_en: Only two people survived the plane crash.
quote_zh: 只有两人在飞机失事中幸存。
ex_en: Harry Potter survived Voldemort's killing curse when he was only one year old.
ex_zh: 哈利·波特在只有一岁时从伏地魔的杀戮咒下幸存下来。

def_en: a small square of fabric with decorative openwork patterns, used for wiping tears or nose
quote_en: She pulled a lace handkerchief from her sleeve.
quote_zh: 她从袖子里抽出一块蕾丝手帕。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall pulled out a lace handkerchief and dabbed at her eyes.
ex_zh: 麦格教授掏出一块蕾丝手帕擦了擦眼睛。

def_en: touched lightly and gently, usually to remove liquid
quote_en: She dabbed at her eyes with a tissue.
quote_zh: 她用纸巾轻轻擦拭眼睛。
ex_en: McGonagall dabbed at her eyes beneath her spectacles.
ex_zh: 麦格在眼镜下面轻轻擦拭眼睛。

def_en: breathed in audibly and deeply through the nose, often to clear it or express emotion
quote_en: He gave a great sniff and wiped his nose.
quote_zh: 他用力吸了一下鼻子，然后擦了擦。
ex_en: Dumbledore gave a great sniff as he took a golden watch from his pocket.
ex_zh: 邓布利多用力吸了一下鼻子，同时从口袋里掏出一块金表。

def_en: looked at carefully and closely
quote_en: The doctor examined the patient's wound.
quote_zh: 医生检查了病人的伤口。
ex_en: Dumbledore examined his odd watch before putting it back in his pocket.
ex_zh: 邓布利多仔细端详了他那块奇怪的手表，然后放回口袋。

def_en: a strange or unusual timepiece
quote_en: His odd watch had planets instead of numbers.
quote_zh: 他那块奇怪的手表上有行星而不是数字。
ex_en: Dumbledore's watch had twelve hands and little planets moving around the edge.
ex_zh: 邓布利多的手表有十二根指针，边缘有小行星在转动。

def_en: twelve pointing needles on a clock or watch (normal watches have three)
quote_en: A normal watch has three hands, but this one had twelve.
quote_zh: 普通手表有三根指针，但这块有十二根。
ex_en: Dumbledore's odd watch had twelve hands but no numbers.
ex_zh: 邓布利多那块奇怪的手表有十二根指针但没有数字。

def_en: small celestial bodies (like stars) circling around the watch face
quote_en: Tiny planets were moving around the edge of the watch.
quote_zh: 微小的行星在表盘边缘转动。
ex_en: Instead of numbers, little planets were moving around the edge of Dumbledore's watch.
ex_zh: 代替数字的是，小行星在邓布利多手表的边缘转动。

def_en: seemed reasonable, understandable, or meaningful to someone
quote_en: The instructions finally made sense to her after the demonstration.
quote_zh: 演示之后她终于看懂了说明。
ex_en: The strange watch must have made sense to Dumbledore, because he put it back.
ex_zh: 那块奇怪的手表一定对邓布利多有意义，因为他把它放回去了。

def_en: standing up quickly from a sitting position
quote_en: She jumped to her feet when she heard the good news.
quote_zh: 听到好消息时她猛地站了起来。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall jumped to her feet and pointed at number four.
ex_zh: 麦格教授猛地站起来，指着四号。

def_en: using one's finger to indicate a direction or object
quote_en: He was pointing at the tallest building in the city.
quote_zh: 他指着城里最高的建筑。
ex_en: "You can't mean the people who live here?" she cried, pointing at number four.
ex_zh: "你不可能指的是住在这里的人吧？"她指着四号喊道。

def_en: more different from us; sharing fewer similarities with us
def_zh: 不像我们；与我们差异更大
quote_en: You couldn't find two people who are less like wizards.
quote_zh: 你找不到比他们更不像巫师的人了。
ex_en: McGonagall said you couldn't find two people who are less like the magical community than the Dursleys.
ex_zh: 麦格说你找不到比德思礼一家更不像魔法界的人了。

def_en: striking his mother with his feet in anger or tantrum
quote_en: The spoiled child was kicking his mother because she wouldn't buy candy.
quote_zh: 那个被宠坏的孩子因为妈妈不给买糖而踢她。
ex_en: McGonagall saw Dudley kicking his mother all the way up the street.
ex_zh: 麦格看到达力一路踢着他妈妈走在街上。

def_en: shouting loudly and demanding candy
quote_en: The toddler was screaming for sweets in the supermarket.
quote_zh: 那个幼儿在超市里尖叫着要糖果。
ex_en: Dudley was screaming for sweets while kicking his mother.
ex_zh: 达力一边踢他妈妈一边尖叫着要糖果。

def_en: in a strong, determined, or confident way
quote_en: She firmly stated that she would not change her mind.
quote_zh: 她坚定地表示不会改变主意。
ex_en: "It's the best place for him," said Dumbledore firmly.
ex_zh: "这是最适合他的地方，"邓布利多坚定地说。

def_en: in a weak, quiet, or barely audible way
quote_en: "I... I don't know," she said faintly.
quote_zh: "我……我不知道，"她微弱地说。
ex_en: "A letter?" repeated Professor McGonagall faintly.
ex_zh: "一封信？"麦格教授无力地重复道。

def_en: someone who is extremely famous and admired, often for extraordinary achievements
quote_en: He became a legend in the world of sports.
quote_zh: 他成为了体育界的传奇人物。
ex_en: McGonagall said Harry would be famous – a legend – and every child would know his name.
ex_zh: 麦格说哈利会出名——成为传奇人物——每个孩子都会知道他的名字。

def_en: a hypothetical holiday named after Harry Potter
quote_en: I wouldn't be surprised if today was known as Harry Potter day in the future.
quote_zh: 如果将来今天被称为哈利·波特日，我不会感到惊讶。
ex_en: McGonagall predicted that the day Voldemort was defeated might become Harry Potter day.
ex_zh: 麦格预言伏地魔被打败的那天可能会成为哈利·波特日。

def_en: to make someone conceited or too proud; to cause someone to become arrogant
def_zh: 让……冲昏头脑；使骄傲自满
quote_en: Winning the competition might turn any young athlete's head.
quote_zh: 赢得比赛可能会让任何年轻运动员冲昏头脑。
ex_en: Dumbledore said that all the fame would be enough to turn any boy's head.
ex_zh: 邓布利多说所有这些名声足以让任何一个男孩冲昏头脑。

word: famous before he can walk and talk
def_en: well-known even before reaching the age of normal development
def_zh: 还不会走路说话就出名了
quote_en: The child actor was famous before he could walk and talk.
quote_zh: 那个童星还不会走路说话就出名了。
ex_en: Harry would be famous for something he won't even remember, before he can walk and talk.
ex_zh: 哈利还不会走路说话就会因为一件他自己都不记得的事情而出名。

def_en: in a more favorable or advantageous situation
quote_en: You would be better off saving your money instead of spending it all.
quote_zh: 你把钱存起来而不是花光会更好。
ex_en: Dumbledore asked McGonagall if she could see how much better off Harry would be, growing up away from fame.
ex_zh: 邓布利多问麦格是否能看到哈利远离名声长大会有多好。

def_en: maturing while separated or distant from something
quote_en: She grew up away from the noise of the big city.
quote_zh: 她远离大城市的喧嚣长大。
ex_en: Harry would grow up away from all the fame until he was ready to take it.
ex_zh: 哈利会远离所有名声长大，直到他准备好面对它。

def_en: prepared or mature enough to handle something
quote_en: He wasn't ready to take on such a big responsibility.
quote_zh: 他还没有准备好承担这么大的责任。
ex_en: Dumbledore wanted Harry to grow up away from fame until he was ready to take it.
ex_zh: 邓布利多希望哈利远离名声长大，直到他准备好面对它。

def_en: looked at his cloak with suspicion or curiosity
quote_en: She eyed his bag suspiciously, wondering what was inside.
quote_zh: 她怀疑地盯着他的包，想知道里面有什么。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall eyed Dumbledore's cloak suddenly, as if she thought Harry might be hiding underneath it.
ex_zh: 麦格教授突然盯着邓布利多的斗篷，好像她以为哈利可能藏在下面。

def_en: concealing something or someone beneath another object
quote_en: The cat was hiding underneath the bed.
quote_zh: 猫藏在床底下。
ex_en: McGonagall thought Dumbledore might be hiding Harry underneath his cloak.
ex_zh: 麦格以为邓布利多可能把哈利藏在他的斗篷下面。

def_en: having or showing good judgment and experience
quote_en: It is wise to save some money for emergencies.
quote_zh: 存些钱以备不时之需是明智的。
ex_en: "You think it wise to trust Hagrid with something as important as this?" asked McGonagall.
ex_zh: "你认为把这么重要的事情交给海格是明智的吗？"麦格问道。

def_en: to give someone responsibility for something valuable or important
def_zh: 把……托付给；信任某人做某事
quote_en: I trust my best friend with my deepest secrets.
quote_zh: 我把最深的秘密托付给我最好的朋友。
ex_en: Dumbledore said he would trust Hagrid with his life.
ex_zh: 邓布利多说他会把自己的生命托付给海格。

def_en: in a reluctant or unwilling way
quote_en: She grudgingly admitted that he was right.
quote_zh: 她不情愿地承认他是对的。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall grudgingly praised Hagrid, saying his heart was in the right place.
ex_zh: 麦格教授不情愿地称赞海格，说他本意是好的。

def_en: not paying enough attention; often making mistakes
quote_en: He lost his keys because he was careless.
quote_zh: 他因为粗心丢了钥匙。
ex_en: McGonagall said Hagrid was careless and tended to make mistakes.
ex_zh: 麦格说海格很粗心，容易出错。

def_en: to often do something or behave in a particular way
quote_en: He tends to arrive late for meetings.
quote_zh: 他开会往往会迟到。
ex_en: "He does tend to – what was that?" McGonagall stopped as she heard a noise.
ex_zh: "他往往会——那是什么声音？"麦格听到声音停了下来。

def_en: a deep, continuous, rolling noise
quote_en: A low rumbling sound came from the distance, like thunder.
quote_zh: 远处传来低沉的隆隆声，像打雷一样。
ex_en: A low rumbling sound broke the silence around them.
ex_zh: 低沉的隆隆声打破了他们周围的寂静。

def_en: ended a period of quiet with a noise
quote_en: The dog's bark broke the silence of the night.
quote_zh: 狗叫声打破了夜晚的寂静。
ex_en: The rumbling sound broke the silence as Dumbledore and McGonagall waited.
ex_zh: 隆隆声打破了邓布利多和麦格等待时的寂静。

def_en: became gradually more intense in volume
quote_en: The music grew steadily louder as they approached the concert hall.
quote_zh: 他们走近音乐厅时，音乐声越来越响。
ex_en: The rumbling sound grew steadily louder until it became a roar.
ex_zh: 隆隆声越来越响，直到变成轰鸣声。

def_en: increased in volume until it became a very loud, deep sound
quote_en: The crowd's cheers swelled to a roar when the team scored.
quote_zh: 当球队得分时，人群的欢呼声增大成一片轰鸣。
ex_en: The sound swelled to a roar as they both looked up at the sky.
ex_zh: 当他们俩都抬头望向天空时，声音增大成了一片轰鸣。

def_en: descended suddenly from the sky, as if dropping
quote_en: The bird fell out of the air after being hit.
quote_zh: 那只鸟被击中后从空中落了下来。
ex_en: A huge motorcycle fell out of the air and landed on the road.
ex_zh: 一辆巨大的摩托车从空中落下来，降落在路上。

def_en: sitting with one leg on each side of something (like a horse or motorcycle)
quote_en: He sat astride the horse, ready to ride.
quote_zh: 他跨坐在马上，准备骑行。
ex_en: The man sitting astride the huge motorcycle was twice as tall as a normal man.
ex_zh: 跨坐在那辆巨大摩托车上的男人比普通人高一倍。

def_en: so enormous that it seems illegal or unbelievable
def_zh: 大得离谱；大得不合规矩
quote_en: The dog was too big to be allowed in the apartment.
quote_zh: 那条狗大得离谱，公寓不让养。
ex_en: Hagrid looked simply too big to be allowed, and so wild.
ex_zh: 海格简直大得不合规矩，而且非常狂野。

def_en: messy, twisted clumps of hair
quote_en: His hair was a mess of long tangles.
quote_zh: 他的头发是乱蓬蓬的一团。
ex_en: Long tangles of bushy black hair and beard hid most of Hagrid's face.
ex_zh: 乱蓬蓬的一团浓密黑发和胡子遮住了海格大部分脸。

def_en: growing thickly and densely (used of hair, fur, or plants)
quote_en: The squirrel had a bushy tail.
quote_zh: 那只松鼠有一条蓬松的大尾巴。
ex_en: Hagrid's bushy black hair and beard hid most of his face.
ex_zh: 海格浓密的黑发和胡子遮住了他大部分脸。

def_en: the removable covers of garbage containers
quote_en: The metal trash can lids clattered on the ground.
quote_zh: 金属垃圾桶盖哐当一声掉在地上。
ex_en: Hagrid's hands were the size of trash can lids.
ex_zh: 海格的双手有垃圾桶盖那么大。

def_en: very large, strong arms with well-developed muscles
def_zh: 巨大而肌肉发达的手臂
quote_en: The weightlifter's vast, muscular arms could lift anything.
quote_zh: 举重运动员巨大而肌肉发达的手臂能举起任何东西。
ex_en: In his vast, muscular arms, Hagrid was holding a bundle of blankets.
ex_zh: 海格巨大而肌肉发达的手臂里抱着一包毯子。

def_en: speaking in a way that shows one is no longer worried
quote_en: "You're safe!" she said, sounding relieved.
quote_zh: "你安全了！"她听起来松了一口气说道。
ex_en: "Hagrid," said Dumbledore, sounding relieved. "At last."
