ex_zh: "海格，"邓布利多听起来松了一口气说道，"终于来了。"

def_en: took and used something temporarily with permission to return it
quote_en: I borrowed a book from the library.
quote_zh: 我从图书馆借了一本书。
ex_en: Hagrid explained that he had borrowed the motorcycle from young Sirius Black.
ex_zh: 海格解释说摩托车是他从小天狼星·布莱克那里借来的。

def_en: gave me something temporarily with the expectation of return
quote_en: My friend lent me his car for the weekend.
quote_zh: 我的朋友把他的车借给我周末用。
ex_en: "Young Sirius Black lent it to me," said Hagrid.
ex_zh: "小天狼星·布莱克借给我的，"海格说。

def_en: rescued or removed someone from a dangerous place
quote_en: The firefighters got the child out of the burning building.
quote_zh: 消防员把孩子从着火的大楼里救了出来。
ex_en: "I got him out all right before the Muggles started swarmin' around."
ex_zh: "在麻瓜开始涌来之前，我把他安全地救了出来。"

def_en: (informal, dialect) gathering or moving in large numbers like insects
quote_en: Reporters started swarmin' around the celebrity.
quote_zh: 记者们开始蜂拥到那位名人周围。
ex_en: Hagrid got Harry out just before the Muggles started swarmin' around the destroyed house.
ex_zh: 海格在麻瓜开始蜂拥到被毁的房子周围之前把哈利救了出来。

def_en: (informal, dialect) flying above or across
quote_en: We were flyin' over the mountains when I saw the sunrise.
quote_zh: 我们飞越山脉时我看到了日出。
ex_en: "He fell asleep as we was flyin' over Bristol," said Hagrid.
ex_zh: "我们飞越布里斯托时他睡着了，"海格说。

def_en: leaned their upper bodies toward something
quote_en: She bent forward to tie her shoelaces.
quote_zh: 她俯身系鞋带。
ex_en: Dumbledore and Professor McGonagall bent forward over the bundle of blankets.
ex_zh: 邓布利多和麦格教授俯身看着那包毯子。

def_en: sleeping deeply
quote_en: The baby was fast asleep in her crib.
quote_zh: 婴儿在她的婴儿床里熟睡。
ex_en: Inside the blankets was a baby boy, fast asleep.
ex_zh: 毯子里面是一个熟睡的男婴。

def_en: a small clump or bunch of hair, grass, or feathers
quote_en: A tuft of hair stuck up from the back of his head.
quote_zh: 一撮头发从他的后脑勺翘起来。
ex_en: Under a tuft of jet-black hair over his forehead was a curious scar.
ex_zh: 在他额前的一撮乌黑头发下面有一道奇特的伤疤。

def_en: extremely black; as black as jet
quote_en: She had long, jet-black hair.
quote_zh: 她有一头乌黑的长发。
ex_en: Harry had a tuft of jet-black hair over his forehead.
ex_zh: 哈利的额前有一撮乌黑的头发。

def_en: a wound or mark with an unusual form
quote_en: The mark on his arm was a curiously shaped cut.
quote_zh: 他手臂上的印记是一道形状奇特的伤口。
ex_en: They could see a curiously shaped cut on Harry's forehead, like a bolt of lightning.
ex_zh: 他们能看到哈利额头上有一道形状奇特的伤口，像一道闪电。

def_en: a flash of lightning, often depicted as a jagged line
quote_en: The scar on Harry's forehead was shaped like a bolt of lightning.
quote_zh: 哈利额头上的伤疤形状像一道闪电。
ex_en: The cut on Harry's forehead was a bolt of lightning shape.
ex_zh: 哈利额头上的伤口是闪电形状。

def_en: be useful or helpful at some time in the future
quote_en: Keep this old rope; it might come in handy someday.
quote_zh: 留着这条旧绳子，总有一天会派上用场。
ex_en: Dumbledore said scars can come in handy, showing his own scar that was a map of the London Underground.
ex_zh: 邓布利多说伤疤能派上用场，并展示了他自己的那块像伦敦地铁地图的伤疤。

def_en: finish something unpleasant or difficult as quickly as possible
def_zh: 把这件事了结；赶紧做完
quote_en: Let's just get this over with so we can relax.
quote_zh: 我们赶紧把这件事了结，这样就能放松了。
ex_en: Dumbledore took Harry and said, "We'd better get this over with."
ex_zh: 邓布利多抱起哈利说："我们最好赶紧把这件事办完。"

def_en: a head covered with long, thick, untidy hair
def_zh: 蓬乱的头；毛发浓密的头
quote_en: The dog shook its shaggy head.
quote_zh: 那只狗摇了摇它那毛茸茸的头。
ex_en: Hagrid bent his great, shaggy head over Harry to say good-bye.
ex_zh: 海格把他那巨大而毛发蓬乱的头俯在哈利上方道别。

def_en: a kiss that feels rough because of facial hair stubble or whiskers
quote_en: The old man's whiskery kiss left red marks on the baby's cheek.
quote_zh: 老人胡子拉碴的吻在婴儿脸颊上留下了红印。
ex_en: Hagrid gave Harry what must have been a very scratchy, whiskery kiss.
ex_zh: 海格给了哈利一个一定很扎人的、胡子拉碜的吻。

def_en: produced a loud, long, crying sound like a wolf or dog
def_zh: 发出一声嚎哭；放声大哭
quote_en: The child let out a howl when he fell off his bike.
quote_zh: 孩子从自行车上摔下来时放声大哭。
ex_en: Suddenly, Hagrid let out a howl like a wounded dog.
ex_zh: 突然，海格像受伤的狗一样嚎啕大哭起来。

def_en: an injured dog, often used to describe a pitiful crying sound
quote_en: The animal made a sound like a wounded dog.
quote_zh: 那只动物发出了像受伤的狗一样的声音。
ex_en: Hagrid's howl was so loud and sad that it sounded like a wounded dog.
ex_zh: 海格的嚎哭声又大又悲伤，听起来像一只受伤的狗。

def_en: made a sharp, sibilant sound like a snake, often to warn or quiet someone
quote_en: "Quiet!" she hissed at the noisy children.
quote_zh: "安静！"她对吵闹的孩子们嘘声道。
ex_en: "Shhh!" hissed Professor McGonagall, "you'll wake the Muggles!"
ex_zh: "嘘！"麦格教授嘶声道，"你会把麻瓜吵醒的！"

def_en: cried noisily while taking in short breaths
quote_en: She sobbed uncontrollably at the funeral.
quote_zh: 她在葬礼上无法控制地抽泣。
ex_en: "S-s-sorry," sobbed Hagrid, burying his face in his handkerchief.
ex_zh: "对——对——对不起，"海格抽噎着说，把脸埋在手帕里。

def_en: a cloth for wiping the nose or tears, decorated with small round marks
quote_en: He pulled a spotted handkerchief from his pocket to wipe his tears.
quote_zh: 他从口袋里掏出一块有斑点的手帕擦眼泪。
ex_en: Hagrid took out a large, spotted handkerchief and buried his face in it.
ex_zh: 海格掏出一块很大的、有斑点的手帕，把脸埋了进去。

def_en: pressing his face deeply into something to hide or comfort himself
quote_en: She was burying her face in her hands, crying.
quote_zh: 她双手掩面哭泣。
ex_en: Hagrid was burying his face in his handkerchief to muffle his sobs.
ex_zh: 海格把脸埋在手帕里以掩盖抽泣声。

def_en: cannot tolerate or endure something emotionally
quote_en: I can't stand it when people are cruel to animals.
quote_zh: 我受不了人们虐待动物。
ex_en: "I c-c-can't stand it – Lily an' James dead," sobbed Hagrid.
ex_zh: "我——我——受不了——莉莉和詹姆死了，"海格抽泣道。

def_en: control your emotions; stop crying or panicking
quote_en: You need to get a grip on yourself and stop crying.
quote_zh: 你需要控制住自己，别再哭了。
ex_en: "Get a grip on yourself, Hagrid, or we'll be found," whispered McGonagall.
ex_zh: "控制住自己，海格，不然我们会被发现的，"麦格低声说。

def_en: in a careful, cautious, or gentle way, often to avoid causing harm
def_zh: 小心翼翼地；轻手轻脚地
quote_en: She gingerly touched the hot pan.
quote_zh: 她小心翼翼地摸了摸热锅。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall patted Hagrid gingerly on the arm.
ex_zh: 麦格教授小心翼翼地拍了拍海格的手臂。

def_en: walked across or over something by lifting one's feet
quote_en: He stepped over the puddle to avoid getting wet.
quote_zh: 他跨过水坑以免弄湿。
ex_en: Dumbledore stepped over the low garden wall and walked to the front door.
ex_zh: 邓布利多跨过低矮的花园围墙，走到前门。

def_en: placed something softly and carefully onto a surface
quote_en: She laid the sleeping baby gently on the bed.
quote_zh: 她把熟睡的婴儿轻轻放在床上。
ex_en: Dumbledore laid Harry gently on the doorstep.
ex_zh: 邓布利多把哈利轻轻放在门阶上。

def_en: placed something securely into a confined space
quote_en: He tucked the note inside the book.
quote_zh: 他把便条塞进书里。
ex_en: Dumbledore tucked the letter inside Harry's blankets.
ex_zh: 邓布利多把信塞进哈利的毯子里。

def_en: for an entire sixty seconds; emphasizing a length of time
quote_en: For a full minute, no one said a word.
quote_zh: 整整一分钟，没有人说一句话。
ex_en: For a full minute the three of them stood and looked at the little bundle.
ex_zh: 整整一分钟，他们三个人站着看着那个小包裹。

def_en: his shoulders moved up and down from crying
quote_en: His shoulders shook as he wept silently.
quote_zh: 他无声地哭泣时肩膀颤抖着。
ex_en: Hagrid's shoulders shook as he tried to hold back his tears.
ex_zh: 海格试图忍住眼泪时肩膀颤抖着。

def_en: blinked repeatedly and rapidly to stop tears from falling
quote_en: She blinked furiously to keep the tears from spilling.
quote_zh: 她拼命眨眼不让眼泪掉下来。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall blinked furiously, trying not to cry.
ex_zh: 麦格教授拼命眨眼，努力不让自己哭出来。

def_en: a sparkling, bright, cheerful shine in someone's eyes
quote_en: The old man's eyes had a friendly twinkling light.
quote_zh: 老人的眼睛里闪烁着友好的光芒。
ex_en: The twinkling light that usually shone from Dumbledore's eyes seemed to have gone out.
ex_zh: 邓布利多眼中通常闪烁的光芒似乎消失了。

def_en: (of light or fire) stopped shining or burning; extinguished
quote_en: The candle flame had gone out.
quote_zh: 蜡烛的火焰熄灭了。
ex_en: For a moment, the kindness in Dumbledore's eyes had gone out, replaced by sadness.
ex_zh: 那一刻，邓布利多眼中的善意消失了，取而代之的是悲伤。

def_en: have no reason or right to remain somewhere
quote_en: You have no business being here after what you did.
quote_zh: 你做了那样的事，没有理由待在这里。
ex_en: Dumbledore said they had no business staying on Privet Drive any longer.
ex_zh: 邓布利多说他们没有理由继续留在女贞路了。

def_en: a voice that sounds unclear because it is covered or dampened
def_zh: 沉闷的声音；含糊的声音
quote_en: He spoke in a muffled voice, his face buried in his hands.
quote_zh: 他脸埋在手里，用沉闷的声音说话。
ex_en: "Yeah," said Hagrid in a very muffled voice, still crying.
ex_zh: "嗯，"海格用非常沉闷的声音说，还在哭。

def_en: eyes from which tears are flowing continuously
quote_en: She came out of the cinema with streaming eyes after the sad movie.
quote_zh: 看完悲伤的电影后，她从电影院出来，泪如泉涌。
ex_en: Hagrid wiped his streaming eyes on his jacket sleeve.
ex_zh: 海格用夹克袖子擦着泪如泉涌的眼睛。

def_en: mounted or climbed onto something with a smooth, swinging motion
quote_en: He swung himself onto the horse and rode away.
quote_zh: 他纵身跨上马，骑马离开了。
ex_en: Hagrid swung himself onto the motorcycle and kicked the engine into life.
ex_zh: 海格纵身跨上摩托车，一脚把发动机蹬燃。

def_en: started the engine by using a kicking motion (as on a motorcycle)
quote_en: He kicked the engine into life and roared off.
quote_zh: 他一脚蹬燃发动机，轰鸣着离开了。
ex_en: With a roar, the motorcycle rose into the air and off into the night.
ex_zh: 随着一声轰鸣，摩托车升入空中，消失在夜色里。

def_en: cleared her nose by forcefully exhaling into a tissue or handkerchief
quote_en: She blew her nose loudly after crying.
quote_zh: 哭完之后她大声地擤了擤鼻涕。
ex_en: Professor McGonagall blew her nose in reply instead of speaking.
ex_zh: 麦格教授没有回答，而是擤了擤鼻子。

def_en: small, glowing spheres of light
quote_en: Twelve balls of light sped back to their street lamps.
quote_zh: 十二个光球飞速返回了各自的路灯。
ex_en: Dumbledore clicked the Put-Outer, and twelve balls of light sped back to the lamps.
ex_zh: 邓布利多按下熄灯器，十二个光球飞速返回了路灯。

def_en: moved quickly to return to a previous location
quote_en: The children sped back home before the rain started.
quote_zh: 孩子们在雨开始之前飞速跑回了家。
ex_en: The balls of light sped back to their street lamps, and Privet Drive glowed orange again.
ex_zh: 光球飞速返回各自的路灯，女贞路重新亮起了橙色的光。

def_en: became illuminated with an orange color all at once
quote_en: The street glowed suddenly orange as the lamps came back on.
quote_zh: 路灯重新亮起时，整条街突然发出橙色的光。
ex_en: Privet Drive glowed suddenly orange as the street lamps reignited.
ex_zh: 路灯重新点亮时，女贞路突然亮起了橙色的光。

def_en: to see or recognize something with difficulty
quote_en: I could barely make out the sign in the fog.
quote_zh: 在雾中我几乎看不清那个牌子。
ex_en: Dumbledore could make out a tabby cat slinking around the corner.
ex_zh: 邓布利多能辨认出一只虎斑猫在拐角处悄悄溜走。

def_en: moving quietly and stealthily, often with a guilty or sneaky appearance
def_zh: 悄悄溜走；鬼鬼祟祟地走动
quote_en: The cat was slinking around the garden, looking for mice.
quote_zh: 那只猫在花园里悄悄溜达，寻找老鼠。
ex_en: Dumbledore could make out a tabby cat slinking around the corner at the other end of the street.
ex_zh: 邓布利多能辨认出一只虎斑猫在街道另一端的拐角处悄悄溜走。

def_en: turned around sharply to face the opposite direction
quote_en: He turned on his heel and walked away without saying goodbye.
quote_zh: 他猛地转身，没说再见就走了。
ex_en: Dumbledore turned on his heel and with a swish of his cloak, he was gone.
ex_zh: 邓布利多猛地转过身，斗篷嗖的一声，他就消失了。

def_en: the soft, sweeping sound made by a cloak when moving quickly
quote_en: The magician disappeared with a swish of his cloak.
quote_zh: 魔术师斗篷嗖的一声就消失了。
ex_en: With a swish of his cloak, Dumbledore was gone.
ex_zh: 斗篷嗖的一声，邓布利多就消失了。

def_en: a gentle wind moved or disturbed something lightly
quote_en: A gentle breeze ruffled the surface of the lake.
quote_zh: 一阵微风拂过湖面。
ex_en: A breeze ruffled the neat hedges of Privet Drive.
ex_zh: 一阵微风吹拂着女贞路整齐的树篱。

def_en: well-trimmed, orderly rows of shrubs
quote_en: The garden was surrounded by neat hedges.
quote_zh: 花园被整齐的树篱环绕。
ex_en: Privet Drive lay silent and tidy under the inky sky, with its neat hedges.
ex_zh: 女贞路在漆黑的天空下安静而整洁地躺着，有着整齐的树篱。

def_en: a sky that is very dark, like black ink
quote_en: The stars shone brightly in the inky sky.
quote_zh: 星星在漆黑的天空中明亮地闪耀。
ex_en: Privet Drive lay silent under the inky sky.
ex_zh: 女贞路在漆黑的天空下安静地躺着。

def_en: the most unlikely or unexpected location
quote_en: This quiet village is the very last place you'd expect a crime to happen.
quote_zh: 这个安静的村庄是你最不可能预料会发生犯罪的地方。
ex_en: Privet Drive was the very last place you would expect astonishing things to happen.
ex_zh: 女贞路是你最不可能预料会发生惊人事件的地方。

def_en: extremely surprising or amazing events
def_zh: 惊人的事情；令人惊讶的事
quote_en: The magician performed astonishing things that no one could explain.
quote_zh: 魔术师表演了无人能解释的惊人事情。
ex_en: Privet Drive was the last place you would expect astonishing things to happen.
ex_zh: 女贞路是你最不可能预料会发生惊人事情的地方。

def_en: turned over while lying down
quote_en: The baby rolled over in his sleep.
quote_zh: 婴儿在睡梦中翻了个身。
ex_en: Harry Potter rolled over inside his blankets without waking up.
ex_zh: 哈利·波特在毯子里翻了个身，没有醒来。

def_en: grasped or held something by wrapping one's hand around it
quote_en: Her small hand closed on the toy.
quote_zh: 她的小手抓住了玩具。
ex_en: One small hand closed on the letter beside him.
ex_zh: 一只小手抓住了身边的信。

def_en: continued sleeping
quote_en: Despite the noise, he slept on.
quote_zh: 尽管有噪音，他还是继续睡。
ex_en: Harry slept on, not knowing he was special or famous.
ex_zh: 哈利继续睡着，不知道自己很特别、很出名。

def_en: poked and squeezed (usually painfully) by someone
quote_en: The older brother prodded and pinched his little sister.
quote_zh: 哥哥戳戳掐掐他的小妹妹。
ex_en: Harry would spend the next few weeks being prodded and pinched by his cousin Dudley.
ex_zh: 接下来的几周，哈利会被他的表哥达力戳戳掐掐。

def_en: raising their drinking glasses as a toast
quote_en: Everyone stood up, holding up their glasses to toast the bride.
quote_zh: 大家都站起来，举起杯子向新娘敬酒。
ex_en: People all over the country were holding up their glasses and saying, "To Harry Potter!"
ex_zh: 全国各地的人们都举起杯子说："敬哈利·波特！"

def_en: very quiet, whispered voices, often out of respect or secrecy
quote_en: They spoke in hushed voices in the library.
quote_zh: 他们在图书馆里轻声细语地说话。
ex_en: People said in hushed voices: "To Harry Potter – the boy who lived!"
ex_zh: 人们压低声音说："敬哈利·波特——大难不死的男孩！"

def_en: the title given to Harry Potter because he survived Voldemort's killing curse
quote_en: Everyone in the wizarding world knew Harry as the boy who lived.
quote_zh: 魔法世界的每个人都知道哈利是"大难不死的男孩"。
ex_en: The chapter ends with people toasting "To Harry Potter – the boy who lived!"
ex_zh: 本章以人们举杯"敬哈利·波特——大难不死的男孩！"结束。
